zh-tw.title: 失戀語錄
zh-tw.description: 從心碎到放下的每一個階段，都需要有人懂。這裡有讓你哭、讓你笑、讓你終於可以呼吸的話。

en.title: Breakup Quotes
en.description: For every stage from heartbreak to letting go — words that let you cry, let you laugh, and finally let you breathe again.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '最好的放下，是不再打擾' format; rewritten to emphasize the internal motive rather than the external silence

zh-tw.content:
真正的放下，不是逼自己不愛，
是不再想跟他說話，也不想證明什麼給他看。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那個還在問「該不該再傳一次訊息」的朋友。重點不是斷聯，是不再需要他的回應。
variations: 釋懷不是把人忘了，是想到他的時候，沒有想再聯絡的衝動。

en.content:
Real letting go isn't forcing yourself to stop loving them.
It's no longer wanting to text, no longer needing to prove anything.
en.editorNote: Send this to the friend who keeps asking 'should I message them just one more time?' The point isn't going silent — it's no longer needing their reply.
variations: Moving on doesn't mean forgetting. It means thinking of them and not reaching for your phone.

items.sourceNote: Original writing on the 凌晨三點傳訊息 universal post-breakup moment; common across multiple Taiwan breakup listicles

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點想傳訊息給他的時候，先去喝一杯水。
醒來的你，會謝謝那個沒按下傳送的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最危險的時間是凌晨。傳給最近剛分手的朋友，當作他半夜手指亂動時的最後一道防線。
variations: 凌晨三點的眼淚不可怕，可怕的是凌晨三點的傳送鍵。

en.content:
When it's 3am and you want to text them, drink a glass of water first.
The you who wakes up will thank the you who didn't hit send.
en.editorNote: Post-breakup, 3am is the danger zone. Send this to a friend who just broke up — it's the last line of defense between their thumbs and the send button.
variations: It's not the 3am tears that hurt. It's the 3am send button.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '有些人只是過客' theme common in Taiwan breakup quotes; original metaphor (借住/鋪床) added

zh-tw.content:
有些人來你的人生，只打算借住一晚。
是你太認真，幫他鋪了一輩子的床。
zh-tw.editorNote: 想通「他從來就沒打算留下」的那一刻，會輕鬆很多。適合傳給那個還在問「我哪裡做錯了」的朋友——他沒做錯，是對方從來沒打算長住。
variations: 你不是輸給對方，你只是把短期關係當長期投資。

en.content:
Some people only check into your life for one night.
The mistake was making the bed like they'd be staying forever.
en.editorNote: Realizing 'they were never planning to stay' lifts a huge weight. Send to the friend still asking 'what did I do wrong' — they didn't. The other person was just never planning to unpack.
variations: You didn't lose to them. You just treated a short-term stay as a long-term investment.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by perspective-resetting breakup quotes; original 段落/封面 metaphor

zh-tw.content:
失去他不是失去全世界。
他只是你人生裡的一個段落，不是封面。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀的時候很容易把整個人生定義在那個人身上。這句話適合貼在房間，提醒自己——他只是其中一頁。
variations: 他是書裡的一個角色，不是作者。你才是。

en.content:
Losing them isn't losing your whole world.
They were a paragraph in your life, not the cover.
en.editorNote: After a breakup it's easy to let one person define your entire story. Stick this on a wall as a reminder — they were just one page.
variations: They were a character in the book, not the author. You are.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '誰失去誰' format common in 76句分手語錄; rewritten for sharper bilateral perspective

zh-tw.content:
你失去的是一個不愛你的人。
他失去的是一個會把他擺在第一順位的人。
誰比較虧，自己算一下。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那種「明明他沒有那麼好，但我就是放不下」的朋友。把帳算清楚有時候比講道理更有效。
variations: 他換掉的是會等他的人，你只是換掉了一個讓你等的人。

en.content:
You lost someone who didn't love you.
They lost someone who would have put them first.
Do the math.
en.editorNote: For the friend stuck on someone who clearly wasn't that great. Sometimes laying out the receipts works better than pep talks.
variations: They traded away someone who would have waited. You only traded away someone who made you wait.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '把愛他的力氣留給自己' theme; rewritten with concrete 滑IG action and outcome

zh-tw.content:
把每天偷滑他IG的力氣，拿去多睡一個小時。
一個月後你會發現，你變好看了，他沒有。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀復原的真理：你花在想他的時間，他不會還給你。適合傳給每天看十次前任限時動態的朋友。
variations: 報復前任最好的方式不是讓他後悔，是讓他發現他根本不知道你過得多好。

en.content:
Take the energy you waste stalking their Instagram and use it to sleep one extra hour.
In a month, you'll glow. They won't.
en.editorNote: Universal post-breakup truth: time you spend thinking about them is time they never give back. For the friend checking their ex's stories ten times a day.
variations: The best revenge isn't making them regret it. It's living so well that they don't even know how good your life got.

items.sourceNote: Original observation on the post-breakup re-evaluation phenomenon; not from a single source

zh-tw.content:
一個人去吃了那家你們以前常去的餐廳。
才發現菜其實沒那麼好吃。
原來不是食物厲害，是你當時愛得用力，連配菜都吃出感情。
zh-tw.editorNote: 釋懷的第一個訊號：你發現之前覺得很特別的東西，原來只是普通。適合傳給那個說「他煮的麵真的不一樣」的朋友。
variations: 等你不愛他了，他的優點會默默消失，你才知道那些優點是你借給他的。

en.content:
Eat at your old spot alone, just once.
The food was actually pretty average.
You weren't tasting the cooking — you were tasting how hard you were loving them at the time.
en.editorNote: First sign of moving on: you realize the things that felt special were actually just ordinary. For the friend who keeps insisting 'but his pasta was different.'
variations: Once you stop loving them, their best qualities quietly disappear. Turns out you were the one lending those qualities to them.

items.sourceNote: Original riff on the 'subscription cancellation' framing of breakups; common humor format

zh-tw.content:
我不是失戀，我只是取消了一個 CP 值很差的訂閱方案。
而且還省下月費。
zh-tw.editorNote: 正在剛分手又被親戚問「怎麼分了」的時候用。把感情當服務帳號處理，瞬間變成消費升級。
variations: 戀愛跟健身房會員一樣，不適合就要勇敢解約，不要心疼前面已經繳的錢。

en.content:
I didn't get dumped. I cancelled a subscription that wasn't worth the monthly fee.
Bonus: I'm saving money now.
en.editorNote: Use this when a relative asks 'why did you two break up?' right after the breakup. Reframing love as a service plan turns sad news into a smart consumer choice.
variations: Relationships are like gym memberships. If it's not working, cancel it. Stop crying about the dues you've already paid.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'red flags' breakup humor format; original 聖誕節裝飾 visual rewrite

zh-tw.content:
現在回頭看，他全身上下都是紅旗。
我當時以為那是聖誕節裝飾。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在好朋友終於分手時，配一杯酒一起說的話。重點是大家都笑了，就代表她真的走出來了。
variations: 戀愛的時候紅旗是浪漫，分手後紅旗才變回紅旗。

en.content:
Looking back, he was waving red flags from every direction.
I thought it was a Christmas display.
en.editorNote: Best delivered to a friend over drinks the night their breakup is final. If everyone laughs, you'll know she's really moving on.
variations: When you're in love, red flags look like romance. After the breakup, they finally look red again.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '2AM Amazon purchase' analogy popular in 2026 English breakup memes; localized to 蝦皮 for Taiwan audience

zh-tw.content:
我選人的眼光，跟我半夜在蝦皮下的單一樣——
當下覺得超讚，隔天早上只想退貨。
zh-tw.editorNote: 戀愛眼光不好沒關係，會自嘲就贏了一半。適合在閨蜜聚會吃飯的時候講，大家都會懂。
variations: 我的戀愛史就是一部「衝動購物退換貨指南」。

en.content:
My taste in partners is like my 2am online shopping.
Felt incredible at the time. Pure regret by morning.
en.editorNote: Bad taste in partners is fine — being able to laugh about it is half the win. Try this one over dinner with friends; everyone will relate.
variations: My dating history is basically a returns and exchanges guide for impulse buys.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by classic 'Ex-pired / Ex-perience / Ex-it' wordplay; rewritten with Taiwan-friendly bilingual punning

zh-tw.content:
前任的英文是 Ex，意思就是已經 Ex-pired，請 Ex-it 我的人生。
感謝你貢獻了這次的 Ex-perience，但我們不接受退款。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合放在 IG 限時動態，宣告新生活開始。雙語雙關讓朋友會心一笑，不會太悲情。
variations: Ex 是過去式，是過期，是出口。三個意思都很適合你。

en.content:
Ex stands for Ex-pired. Time to Ex-it.
Thanks for the Ex-perience — sorry, no refunds available.
en.editorNote: Perfect for an IG story announcing a fresh start. The wordplay keeps it light without sounding bitter.
variations: Ex means past tense, expired, and exit. All three definitions apply.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '不是每段相遇都有結局' theme common in zh-tw breakup listicles; rewritten with practical learning angle

zh-tw.content:
不是每段感情都需要有結局。
有些只是來告訴你：下一個，要避開哪一種人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把失戀當成「資料庫更新」就沒那麼難過了。適合傳給連續被同類型的人傷的朋友——這次不是運氣差，是上次教會你了。
variations: 每一次失戀都是一張地圖，標記著下次不要走的路。

en.content:
Not every relationship needs a happy ending.
Some are just here to teach you which kind of person to avoid next time.
en.editorNote: Treating breakups as 'database updates' takes the sting out. For the friend who keeps falling for the same type — this time wasn't bad luck, it was last time's lesson.
variations: Every breakup is a map marking the roads you won't take next time.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '我們都很好，只是時間不湊巧' canonical line; rewritten with 學會愛人/在不在這一頁 framing

zh-tw.content:
我們不是不夠喜歡，是時間從來沒對過。
你出現的時候我還在學怎麼愛人，等我學會了，你已經不在這一頁了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在多年後想起某個人時用。不是誰的錯，就是時間的問題——說出來會比一直放在心裡輕。
variations: 有些人不是不愛你，只是來得太早，或是你成熟得太晚。

en.content:
It wasn't that we didn't love each other enough.
The timing was just never right. By the time I learned how to love properly, you'd already turned to a different page.
en.editorNote: For when someone from years ago crosses your mind. Nobody's fault — it was just timing. Saying it out loud feels lighter than keeping it in.
variations: Some people don't fail to love you. They just show up too early, or you grow up too late.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '愛得多深就有多痛' theme; rewritten as permission to validate one's own sincerity

zh-tw.content:
失戀有多痛，就證明你當時愛得有多認真。
別為自己的真心道歉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那種會說「都怪我太傻」的朋友。她不傻，她只是真的愛過——而那從來不是錯。
variations: 會痛是因為你願意打開，這從來不是缺點。

en.content:
However much it hurts, that's how real your love was.
Don't apologize for meaning it.
en.editorNote: For the friend who keeps saying 'I was so stupid.' She wasn't stupid — she just loved for real. That's never been the wrong answer.
variations: It hurts because you let yourself open up. That has never been a weakness.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '謝謝你的離開' theme common in 致前任 letters; rewritten with 終於有時間照顧自己 angle

zh-tw.content:
謝謝你選擇離開。
我終於有時間好好照顧自己——
才發現原來我這麼好，當初是真的虧待你了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 重新發現自己有多好，比任何挽回都治癒。適合傳給那個「離開後反而活得更開」的朋友互相確認。
variations: 他離開以後我反而開始喜歡自己，這大概是他這輩子做過最好的事。

en.content:
Thanks for choosing to leave.
I finally have time to take care of myself —
turns out I'm pretty great. You really got the better end of that deal.
en.editorNote: Rediscovering how great you are heals more than any reunion would. Send it to a friend who's somehow living better since the breakup — it's worth saying out loud.
variations: I started liking myself after they left. Honestly, leaving was the best thing they ever did for me.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '此生再也不見' farewell format; rewritten with mutual pretending closure

zh-tw.content:
下次在路上遇到，我會假裝已經忘了你。
你也假裝沒看見，我們曾經那麼喜歡過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 成熟版的「再也不見」。不是恨，也不挽留，就是兩個人安靜地把那段關係收進抽屜裡。
variations: 最體面的結束，是兩個人都決定不提了。

en.content:
Next time we cross paths, I'll pretend I've forgotten you.
Pretend you don't remember either — that we ever meant it.
en.editorNote: The grown-up version of 'goodbye forever.' No anger, no hoping. Two people quietly tucking the relationship into a drawer and closing it.
variations: The most graceful ending is when both people just stop bringing it up.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I didn't lose, I outgrew' empowerment frame; rewritten with 鞋子/腳 metaphor

zh-tw.content:
不是他離開你，是你長大了，他變成了不合腳的鞋。
硬穿只會走一步痛一步。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給「明明在一起好幾年，怎麼突然就不適合」的朋友。不是感情變了，是你變了，這從來不是壞事。
variations: 你沒有被丟下，你只是長到他裝不下你的程度。

en.content:
They didn't leave you. You grew up, and they became the shoes that no longer fit.
Forcing them on just hurts every step.
en.editorNote: For the friend wondering 'we were together for years, how did this stop fitting?' The love didn't change — you did, and that's never the wrong thing.
variations: You weren't left behind. You just grew past the size they could hold.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'silence is peace' healing theme; rewritten with WiFi 訊號 modern metaphor

zh-tw.content:
他不回訊息的時候，我以為是世界末日。
現在我手機關靜音一整天，反而覺得心裡有 WiFi 滿格的安靜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 從「等他回訊息」到「不在乎他回不回」，是失戀復原的里程碑。傳給那個終於把通知關掉的朋友。
variations: 以前怕他不讀我，現在我連他傳什麼都懶得看。這就是進步。

en.content:
When he didn't reply, I thought it was the end of the world.
Now I leave my phone on silent all day and feel like my head has full-bar Wi-Fi quiet.
en.editorNote: Going from 'waiting for his reply' to 'not caring if he replies' is a real recovery milestone. For the friend who finally muted his notifications.
variations: I used to panic when he left me on read. Now I don't even open what he sends. That's growth.

items.sourceNote: Original riff on the 'graduated from toxic relationships' joke; localized with 學歷/畢業證書 framing

zh-tw.content:
我不是被分手，是從這段感情畢業了。
學歷：戀愛系，主修踩雷，副修自我懷疑。
下一站：實習人生，地點：自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把分手當畢業是最解氣的詮釋。適合分手後第一次出去聚會時拿出來，朋友會大笑然後幫你倒酒。
variations: 畢業證書上寫著：恭喜本人完成戀愛實境秀，學分修完，可以下台一鞠躬。

en.content:
I didn't get dumped. I graduated from this relationship.
Major: dating disasters. Minor: self-doubt.
Next stop: interning at being myself.
en.editorNote: Reframing a breakup as graduation feels great. Pull this one out at your first post-split hangout — friends will laugh, then top up your glass.
variations: My diploma reads: congratulations on completing the reality show. Credits earned. Time to bow out.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'comeback story' empowerment quotes; rewritten with 章節/作者 control metaphor

zh-tw.content:
他不愛你，不是你的劇情爛，是他看不懂你這本書。
換個讀者，故事繼續，主角從來沒下台。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀最大的誤會是以為「沒人愛＝自己不夠好」。其實只是讀錯讀者。傳給那個開始懷疑自己的朋友，提醒她她還是主角。
variations: 你不需要每個人都看懂你，你只需要寫下去。

en.content:
They didn't love you because they couldn't read your book — not because the story was bad.
Find a new reader. The plot keeps going. The lead never leaves the stage.
en.editorNote: The biggest breakup lie is 'no one loves me, so I'm not enough.' Wrong reader, that's all. For the friend starting to doubt herself — remind her she's still the main character.
variations: You don't need everyone to understand you. You just need to keep writing.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '披薩比愛情可靠' humor frame popular in 2026 IG captions; rewritten with 鹹酥雞 Taiwan localization

zh-tw.content:
我不需要一個會說早安的男友。
我需要一份不會冷掉的鹹酥雞，而且它從來不會已讀我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後第一週最有效的療法不是道理，是宵夜。傳給剛分手的閨蜜，附上一張鹹酥雞的照片效果更好。
variations: 感情可能會背叛你，但鹹酥雞絕對不會。
下一段感情我希望對方至少有鹹酥雞那麼準時送到。

en.content:
I don't need a boyfriend who texts good morning.
I need fried chicken that doesn't get cold, and it never leaves me on read.
en.editorNote: Best post-breakup remedy in the first week isn't wisdom — it's late-night snacks. Send to a freshly single friend with a photo of takeout attached.
variations: Love might betray you. Fried chicken never will.
Next time I'll settle for someone who arrives on time, just like the delivery guy.

items.sourceNote: Original take on 'glow up post-breakup' theme; rewritten with 鏡子/濾鏡 self-recognition angle

zh-tw.content:
分手後第三十天，我在鏡子裡看到自己笑了。
沒有開美顏，沒有打燈，是真的好看。
原來我整個人都是濾鏡，他只是擋光的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後突然發現自己變好看，其實不是真的變了，是擋住光的人不在了。適合貼在貼文裡，配一張素顏自拍。
variations: 他離開以後我才發現，原來我自己就是場景。
你以為他是你的光，後來才知道他是你的影子。

en.content:
Day thirty after the breakup, I caught myself smiling in the mirror.
No filter, no good lighting — just genuinely glowing.
Turns out I was the filter all along. He was just blocking the light.
en.editorNote: When you suddenly look better post-breakup, you didn't change — the person blocking your light just left. Pair this with a no-makeup selfie for the post.
variations: After they left, I realized I was the whole scenery.
You thought they were your light. Turns out they were your shadow.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '時間治癒一切' classic theme; rewritten with 預售屋/交屋 modern Taiwan property metaphor

zh-tw.content:
釋懷不會在某一天突然發生。
它像預售屋一樣，慢慢蓋，等你某天經過才發現——
咦，已經可以交屋了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 復原不是一個瞬間，是一個過程。傳給那個一直問「要多久才會好」的朋友——不知道，但有一天你會自己發現。
variations: 你不會記得自己是哪一天放下的，只會某天突然意識到，他已經不痛了。
釋懷是後照鏡，等你回頭看才發現自己已經開很遠了。

en.content:
Letting go doesn't happen on one specific day.
It's built slowly, like a house under construction. One day you walk past and realize —
wait, I can move in now.
en.editorNote: Recovery isn't a moment, it's a process. For the friend who keeps asking 'how long will this take?' — nobody knows, but one day you'll just notice it's done.
variations: You won't remember which day you let go. You'll just notice one day that thinking of them doesn't hurt anymore.
Moving on is a rearview mirror. You only see how far you've come when you look back.

items.sourceNote: Original riff on the 'unfollow but make it spiritual' meme; rewritten with 雲端硬碟 storage metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我沒有刪掉你的照片，只是把它們搬到雲端硬碟最深的資料夾。
不是捨不得，是因為刪除鍵按下去太用力，會被別人聽到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 成熟版的「我已經沒事了」。其實還沒完全好，但已經懂得不讓別人知道。傳給那種裝沒事但你看得出來的朋友。
variations: 我不是還愛你，我只是還沒空整理硬碟。
封存不等於刪除，但已經比放在桌面上進步很多了。

en.content:
I didn't delete your photos. I just moved them to the deepest folder in my cloud drive.
Not because I can't let go — pressing delete too hard makes a sound, and I don't want anyone to hear it.
en.editorNote: The grown-up version of 'I'm totally fine.' Not actually fine, but learning not to broadcast it. For the friend who's pretending well but you can tell.
variations: I don't still love you. I just haven't gotten around to cleaning my drive.
Archiving isn't deleting — but it's a big step up from leaving them on the desktop.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '披薩外送員比男友可靠' humor format; rewritten with Taiwan 外送 30 分鐘準時 angle

zh-tw.content:
我不需要白馬王子，我只需要 foodpanda 三十分鐘內送到。
至少他不會遲到，也不會說「在忙」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最實際的真理：能準時的人比會說情話的人珍貴一百倍。傳給那個還在等對方回訊息的朋友。
variations: 對方可能會已讀不回，但外送員一定會打給你。
下一段感情我希望對方至少有外送員的職業道德。

en.content:
I don't need a knight in shining armor. I just need a delivery guy who shows up in thirty minutes.
At least he won't be late or say 'I'm busy right now.'
en.editorNote: Most practical post-breakup truth: punctual people are worth a hundred sweet talkers. For the friend still waiting on a reply.
variations: He might leave you on read, but the delivery driver will always call.
Next time I just want a partner with the work ethic of a takeout courier.

items.sourceNote: Original take on the 'solo chapter' empowerment frame; rewritten with 主角光環/獨角戲 Taiwan-friendly metaphor

zh-tw.content:
從「我們」變回「我」之後，主角光環反而更亮了。
原來雙人舞跳不好，是因為這支本來就該是獨角戲。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最大的驚喜：你發現自己一個人比兩個人更完整。傳給那個剛恢復單身、開始重新認識自己的朋友。
variations: 回到一個人不是後退，是回到原本的位置。
兩個人的時候我在配合節奏，一個人才終於跳出自己的舞步。

en.content:
Going from 'us' back to 'me,' my main character glow actually got brighter.
Maybe the duet kept failing because it was always meant to be a solo.
en.editorNote: Biggest post-breakup surprise: you find yourself more whole alone than you ever were as a pair. For the friend rediscovering herself in fresh single life.
variations: Going back to one isn't a step backward — it's returning to your original position.
With them, I was matching their rhythm. Alone, I finally hear my own.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '走進你的世界教你說再見' theme; rewritten with 字幕組/翻譯 modern metaphor

zh-tw.content:
有些人不是來陪你過一輩子的，是來幫你翻譯「什麼叫不適合」。
看懂之後，他就下檔了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把每段感情當成「教學影片」就不那麼難過了。傳給那個一直問「為什麼是我」的朋友——不是你被選中受傷，是你被選中學會。
variations: 他是字幕組，不是主角。把你看不懂的事翻成中文之後就消失了。
有些人是工具人，幫你工具自己長大。

en.content:
Some people aren't here to spend a lifetime with you. They're here to translate the phrase 'not the right fit.'
Once you understand the subtitle, they exit the screen.
en.editorNote: Treating every relationship as a 'tutorial video' takes the sting out. For the friend asking 'why me?' — you weren't chosen to suffer, you were chosen to learn.
variations: They were the subtitle team, not the lead. They translated what you couldn't read, then quietly logged off.
Some people are tools — tools you use to grow yourself up.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'breaking free, shining brighter' empowerment frame; rewritten with 體重計/負重 weight-loss metaphor

zh-tw.content:
分手後我站上體重計，數字沒變，可是身體輕了好多。
原來扛在心上的東西，從來不會被秤到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 心理重量比體重更真實。傳給那個分手後突然覺得「呼吸變順」的朋友——那不是錯覺，那是放下的重量。
variations: 瘦下來最快的方式不是運動，是放下一個不愛你的人。
他離開以後，我才知道原來我背了一個人那麼久。

en.content:
After the breakup I stepped on the scale. The number didn't move, but my body felt lighter.
Turns out the things you carry in your heart never show up in pounds.
en.editorNote: Emotional weight is more real than body weight. For the friend who suddenly says 'I can breathe now' post-breakup — that's not your imagination, that's the weight letting go.
variations: Fastest way to lose weight isn't exercise. It's putting down someone who doesn't love you.
After they left, I realized I'd been carrying a whole person on my back.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'bad credit / poor return' breakup humor format; rewritten with 信用卡停卡 Taiwan-friendly metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我這次不是失戀，是把那張信用爛掉的卡停掉。
額度沒幾塊，利息倒是收得很勤——還是停卡比較省心。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把感情當金融商品看，分手就變成理性決策。傳給那個還在猶豫要不要回頭的朋友——別再給沒信用的人額度了。
variations: 他不是壞人，他只是一張信用分數很低的卡。
感情就像分期付款，剩下的別硬還，提前結清比較划算。

en.content:
This wasn't a breakup. I just cancelled a credit card with terrible credit.
Barely any limit, but the interest was relentless — easier to just close the account.
en.editorNote: Treating love like a financial product turns breakups into rational decisions. For the friend on the fence about going back — stop extending credit to people who never paid up.
variations: He's not a bad guy. He's just a card with a really low credit score.
Relationships are like installment plans. Don't keep paying — settle early and save yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'planting seeds of self-love' empowerment frame popular in 2026 captions; rewritten with 重灌系統 / 開機 tech metaphor

zh-tw.content:
分手不是當機，是重灌系統。
刪掉的不是你，是那些拖慢你的程式。
開機之後，跑得比以前都還順。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀像重開機，有時候反而是更新版本的契機。傳給那個剛分手又開始懷疑自己的朋友，提醒她系統好著呢，只是清掉了垃圾。
variations: 你不是壞了，你只是需要重新開機。
封存舊版本，下載新自己——空間比想像中大很多。

en.content:
A breakup isn't a system crash. It's a clean reinstall.
What got deleted wasn't you — it was the apps slowing you down.
Now you're booting up faster than ever.
en.editorNote: Breakups are like rebooting — sometimes they're really an upgrade. For the freshly single friend doubting herself: the system's fine, you just cleared the junk.
variations: You're not broken. You just needed to restart.
Archive the old version. Download a new self. The storage was bigger than you thought.

items.sourceNote: Original riff on 'choose my peace' empowerment line; rewritten with 行事曆 / 空白格 daily-life Taiwan metaphor

zh-tw.content:
以前我的行事曆都是配合他的時間，
現在每一格都寫著我自己。
空白也是我的，不再是等他出現的位置。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最爽的事不是有空，是發現「空」原來是自由。傳給那個剛把行事曆清空、開始重新填的朋友。
variations: 我以前的時間表是雙人房，現在是套房，視野比較好。
原來等一個人的位置空下來，是多了一塊可以呼吸的地方。

en.content:
My calendar used to be built around his schedule.
Now every slot has my name on it.
Even the blank squares are mine — not seats saved for someone who never showed.
en.editorNote: The best part of a breakup isn't free time — it's realizing free time is freedom. For the friend who just wiped her calendar and started filling it for herself.
variations: My old schedule was a shared room. Now it's a studio with a better view.
Turns out the seat I kept saving for him was just space I could finally breathe in.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'starting fresh feels stronger' healing frame; rewritten with 第二集 / 編劇 Taiwan drama metaphor

zh-tw.content:
他把我的故事寫到一半就走了，
我以為自己是棄稿，後來才發現——
這不是被砍掉，是換我自己當編劇。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀最大的轉折是從「被寫」變成「自己寫」。傳給那個總覺得「沒有他我寫不下去」的朋友——你才是作者，他只是前一集的角色。
variations: 他下檔了，主角還沒下班。
上一集是愛情劇，下一集我自己決定要拍什麼類型。

en.content:
He stopped writing my story halfway and walked off set.
I thought I was a scrapped draft — turns out —
I wasn't cancelled. I just took over as the writer.
en.editorNote: The biggest plot twist after a breakup is moving from 'being written' to 'writing yourself.' For the friend who feels she can't continue without him — you're the author. He was just a season-one character.
variations: He's off the air. The lead is still clocked in.
Last season was a romance. This season, I pick the genre.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'plot twist: I'm better off alone' 2026 caption trend; rewritten with 主角換人 / 反派下線 Taiwan drama framing

zh-tw.content:
劇情大轉折：原來我不是配角，是主角自己。
反派下線之後，整部戲的節奏才終於對了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後重新發現「我才是主角」的瞬間最爽。傳給那個剛意識到自己一直在配合別人演出的朋友。
variations: 他下線之後，我的劇情突然很好看。
原來不是劇本爛，是有人一直亂改台詞。

en.content:
Plot twist: I wasn't the supporting cast. I was the lead all along.
Once the villain logged off, the pacing of the whole show finally clicked.
en.editorNote: The post-breakup high of realizing 'I was the main character' is unmatched. For the friend who just noticed she'd been adjusting her lines for someone else's show.
variations: After they exited, my storyline suddenly got good.
The script wasn't bad. Someone was just rewriting my lines.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'the cure for a broken heart is self-love' theme; rewritten with 處方箋 / 自我照顧 medical metaphor

zh-tw.content:
心碎沒有特效藥。
醫生開的處方是：早睡、好好吃飯、把愛他的力氣分一半給自己。
副作用是——你會發現自己原來這麼值得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀復原沒有捷徑，但有方向。傳給那個還在問「我什麼時候才會好」的朋友——從今晚早點睡開始。
variations: 治療失戀的方法不是換一個人，是找回自己。
把那些等他回訊息的時間，拿來陪自己吃一頓好飯。

en.content:
There's no instant cure for heartbreak.
The prescription is: sleep early, eat properly, give yourself half the love you wasted on them.
Side effect — you realize how worthy you actually are.
en.editorNote: No shortcuts to healing, but there's a direction. For the friend asking 'when will I feel better?' — start by going to bed early tonight.
variations: The cure for heartbreak isn't replacing them. It's finding yourself again.
Take all those hours you spent waiting on a reply and treat yourself to a proper meal.

items.sourceNote: Original riff on 'dodged a bullet' breakup humor; rewritten with 抽到 SSR / 課金 gacha game metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我以為他是 SSR，沒想到是課金課了一年才發現的限定垃圾。
還好我退坑得早，下一個遊戲說不定能抽到真的好東西。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把感情當抽卡看待最不傷自尊。傳給那種「為什麼花這麼多時間在他身上」自責的朋友——課金都會後悔，重點是你願意退坑。
variations: 他不是抽卡保底，他是新手陷阱。
感情不是課金越多越值得，是該停就停。

en.content:
I thought he was a legendary pull. Turned out he was a limited-edition flop I'd been spending on for a year.
Good thing I quit early. Maybe the next game has actually rare drops.
en.editorNote: Treating love like gacha keeps your dignity intact. For the friend beating herself up over 'why did I waste so much time?' — every spender regrets eventually. The point is quitting.
variations: He wasn't the guaranteed pull. He was the rookie trap.
Love isn't worth more the more you spend. Sometimes the right move is just to stop.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'closure isn't a conversation, it's a choice' modern healing frame; rewritten with 結案報告 / 自己簽名 office metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我等了好久他給我一個交代，後來才懂——
結案報告不用等他簽名，我自己蓋章就生效了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀最大的誤會是「需要對方給答案」。其實答案一直在你手上。傳給那個還在等對方解釋的朋友——別等了，你自己就是窗口。
variations: 釋懷不是等他開口，是你自己決定不再追問。
他不會給你答案，因為這個案子早就轉到你的桌上了。

en.content:
I waited forever for him to give me closure. Then I finally got it —
the case report doesn't need his signature. My stamp alone makes it official.
en.editorNote: The biggest breakup myth is 'I need their explanation to move on.' The answer was always yours. For the friend still waiting on a final talk — stop waiting. You're the only office that matters.
variations: Closure isn't them speaking. It's you deciding to stop asking.
He won't hand you an answer. The file got transferred to your desk a long time ago.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'plot twist: I'm the best thing that never happened to him' savage caption format; rewritten with 限量商品 / 售完不補 retail metaphor

zh-tw.content:
他說我會後悔放手，可是我這款限量發行，售完不補。
後悔的從來不是我，是他下次還想下單的時候。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最有底氣的回應，就是知道自己有多稀有。傳給那個被前任威脅「沒有我你會後悔」的朋友——他才會。
variations: 我不是斷貨，是只此一檔，他錯過了就是錯過了。
後悔是他的事，活得好是我的事。

en.content:
He said I'd regret letting go. But I'm a limited edition — once it's gone, it's gone.
The one with regret won't be me. It'll be him, the next time he wants to add to cart.
en.editorNote: The most grounded post-breakup energy comes from knowing your own rarity. For the friend whose ex pulled the 'you'll regret this' line — no, he will.
variations: I'm not out of stock. I was a one-time release. He missed it, that's on him.
Regret is his problem. Living well is mine.

items.sourceNote: Original take on the 'better off alone' empowerment frame; rewritten with 單人套餐 / 加大份量 Taiwan food-court metaphor

zh-tw.content:
以前我點雙人套餐，總覺得要分一半給他才夠意思。
現在我點單人套餐升級加大，整份都是我的——
才發現原來我一直在餓著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 戀愛裡最容易忘記的事，是先把自己餵飽。傳給那個總是把好的留給對方、自己吃剩的朋友——這次點滿你想吃的。
variations: 一個人吃飯不可憐，可憐的是兩個人吃飯還餓著。
我終於不用問他要不要分，因為這份從頭到尾都是我的。

en.content:
I used to order the couple's combo and feel obliged to split it with him.
Now I order the single, sized up — the whole tray is mine.
Turns out I'd been hungry the whole time.
en.editorNote: The thing love makes you forget: feed yourself first. For the friend who always saved the good bites for him and ate the leftovers — order the full plate this time.
variations: Eating alone isn't sad. What's sad is eating with someone and still going hungry.
I don't have to ask if he wants a bite anymore. The whole plate has been mine all along.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '換個時間結局一定會不同' classic Taiwan breakup line; rewritten with 紅綠燈 / 班次 transit metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我們不是錯的人，只是錯的班次。
你那班車剛走的時候，我才剛走到月台。
我們都沒遲到，只是時刻表不一樣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 有些人不是不愛，是時間軸沒對上。傳給那個一直怪自己「為什麼不早一點遇見」的朋友——你準時，他也準時，只是你們的時刻表不同。
variations: 我趕到的時候，你的車已經開走了。不是誰的錯，只是時間沒給我們同一班。
對的人坐錯班次，比錯的人坐對班次還難過。

en.content:
We weren't the wrong people. We just caught the wrong train.
Yours had just pulled out as I made it to the platform.
Neither of us was late — our schedules were just different.
en.editorNote: Sometimes the love was real, the timing wasn't. For the friend blaming herself with 'why didn't I meet him sooner' — you were on time. He was on time. Different timetables.
variations: By the time I arrived, your train had already left. No one's fault — the schedule just never matched.
The right person on the wrong train hurts more than the wrong person on the right one.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'turn this breakup into a step-up' 2026 caption trend; rewritten with 健身 / 重訓 progression metaphor

zh-tw.content:
心碎就像重訓，當下覺得快不行了，
結果隔天起來，你會發現心臟比昨天更壯一點。
他不是讓你受傷的人，是幫你加重量的訓練機。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀痛是真的，但你會變強也是真的。傳給那個剛分手覺得自己「再也撐不住」的朋友——這次的痛，是下次的肌肉。
variations: 失戀像深蹲，腿會痠，但你之後跑得比較快。
他離開的那天我以為自己壞了，後來才發現只是在練心臟。

en.content:
Heartbreak is like lifting weights. In the moment it feels like you can't take it,
but the next morning your heart is a little stronger than yesterday.
He wasn't the one who hurt you. He was just the machine that added the load.
en.editorNote: The pain is real, and so is the strength you build. For the friend who just split and feels she 'can't take any more' — today's pain is tomorrow's muscle.
variations: Heartbreak is a squat session. Your legs ache, but you run faster after.
I thought I was broken the day he left. Turns out I was just training the heart muscle.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'the trash took itself out' savage caption format; rewritten with 垃圾車音樂 / 少女的祈禱 Taiwan-only auditory cue

zh-tw.content:
我沒有把他丟掉，是他自己跟著垃圾車走的。
從那天起我聽到《少女的祈禱》都覺得是慶祝音樂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把分手當垃圾車經過，從此給自己一個輕鬆的儀式感。傳給那個剛甩掉爛人、需要一點黑色幽默的朋友。
variations: 他不是被我丟掉，他是分類正確自己滾走的。
我家附近的垃圾車一週來三次，比他還準時。

en.content:
I didn't throw him out. He left on his own — right behind the garbage truck.
Ever since, the truck's jingle has sounded like a celebration song.
en.editorNote: Reframe the breakup as the trash collecting itself, and you've got a built-in ritual. For the friend who finally took out the garbage and needs some dark humor.
variations: I didn't dump him. He sorted himself into the right bin.
The garbage truck shows up three times a week. More reliable than he ever was.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'champagne / cup of tea' upgrade savage line; rewritten with 全聯 / 微風 Taiwan retail tier metaphor

zh-tw.content:
他覺得我會回頭，可是我已經升級了——
以前我在全聯找特價，現在我在微風試衣服。
等級不一樣了，不好意思。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最甜的反擊不是罵他，是過得比他更好。傳給那個前任突然又來敲門的朋友——禮貌已讀就好。
variations: 他不是我前任，他是我消費升級前的舊版本。
我沒漲價，是他預算根本不夠。

en.content:
He thinks I'll come back, but I've leveled up.
I used to clip coupons at the discount mart. Now I'm trying things on at the boutique.
Different tier. Sorry not sorry.
en.editorNote: The sweetest post-breakup revenge isn't trash talk — it's just living better. For the friend whose ex suddenly slid back in: a polite read receipt is enough.
variations: He's not my ex. He's just the older version of me before I upgraded.
I didn't raise my price. His budget was never going to cover it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '放下不是忘記' classic theme common in zh-tw 放下感情語錄; rewritten with 收件匣 / 封存 inbox metaphor

zh-tw.content:
放下不是把他從記憶裡刪掉，
是把他從收件匣移到封存夾。
還在那裡，但你不會每天打開了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 釋懷最務實的版本：不否認他存在過，只是不再優先處理。傳給那個還在「該不該完全忘記」的朋友——封存就夠了。
variations: 封存不是放棄，是你終於不用每天看到他的通知。
回憶可以留著，只是不用釘選在最上面。

en.content:
Letting go isn't deleting them from your memory.
It's moving them from inbox to archive.
Still there, but you stop opening it every day.
en.editorNote: The most realistic version of moving on: not denying they existed, just not prioritizing them anymore. For the friend torn over 'should I forget completely' — archive is enough.
variations: Archive isn't surrender. It just means you stop getting daily notifications.
You can keep the memories. You just don't have to pin them to the top.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'plot twist: I'm the best thing that never happened to him' savage frame; rewritten with 試用版 / 沒付費 SaaS metaphor

zh-tw.content:
他用了我七個月的試用版，沒升級會員就跑了。
沒關係，付費功能他這輩子都看不到了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把自己當付費版軟體，分手變成他錯過的版本更新。傳給那個被「不珍惜」對待的朋友——下次只開放給願意訂閱的人。
variations: 他享受了免費版，現在連登入頁面都進不來了。
我不是降價，是直接停止對他開放。

en.content:
He used the seven-month free trial, then bailed before upgrading.
That's fine. He'll never see the premium features now.
en.editorNote: Treat yourself like paid software and a breakup becomes the update he missed. For the friend who got 'not appreciated' — next time, only let subscribers in.
variations: He enjoyed the free tier. Now he can't even reach the login page.
I didn't lower the price. I just closed the account to him.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I'm not a snack, I'm a whole meal' savage caption frame; rewritten with 便當 / 加菜 Taiwan lunchbox metaphor

zh-tw.content:
他以為我是配菜，可以挑掉不吃。
抱歉，我是整個便當，連白飯都比他有飽足感。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最爽的覺悟是「我從來就不是 side dish」。傳給那種總是在感情裡被當備胎、被挑掉的朋友——你是主菜，不是裝飾。
variations: 他不是不愛我，他只是分量太小裝不下我。
下次點餐我自己決定要哪幾道，不再讓人幫我配。

en.content:
He thought I was a side dish he could push to the edge of the plate.
Sorry — I'm the whole bento, and even the rice is more filling than he was.
en.editorNote: The best post-breakup realization is 'I was never a side dish.' For the friend who always got treated like a backup option — you're the main course, not the garnish.
variations: It's not that he didn't love me. His portion was just too small to hold me.
Next time I'm picking the menu myself. No one's plating me.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '心痛是過程不是結果' classic theme; rewritten with 天氣預報 / 颱風 Taiwan weather metaphor

zh-tw.content:
心碎不是天災，是天氣。
會有颱風、會有低氣壓，但不會永遠下雨。
你只是還沒等到放晴的那天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀的時候很容易誤以為「永遠走不出來」。其實心情跟天氣一樣，再爛也會過去。傳給那個還在低氣壓裡的朋友——撐傘就好，不用搬家。
variations: 今天心情有雨，明天可能就大太陽。別在雨天決定整個人生。
再大的颱風都會有眼，再痛的失戀都會有空檔讓你呼吸。

en.content:
Heartbreak isn't a natural disaster. It's just weather.
There'll be typhoons, there'll be low-pressure days, but it won't rain forever.
You just haven't reached the day it clears up yet.
en.editorNote: Mid-heartbreak it's easy to think 'I'll never feel okay again.' Moods are like weather though — even the worst storms move on. For the friend stuck under the gray sky: just grab an umbrella, don't pack up your life.
variations: Today's forecast is rain. Tomorrow might be sun. Don't make life decisions on a rainy day.
Even the worst typhoon has an eye. Even the worst heartbreak has moments to breathe.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'glow up, level up' empowerment caption frame; rewritten with 隊友 / 開外掛 gaming metaphor

zh-tw.content:
之前打副本一直死，我以為是我太菜。
換隊友後才發現——是上一個一直拖我後腿，還不肯下線。
單刷反而過關了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 有些感情不是你不夠強，是隊友選錯。傳給那個一直覺得「是不是我的問題」的朋友——你沒問題，是組隊邏輯有問題。
variations: 我以前過不了的關，現在自己一個人通了。原來他才是 boss。
你不是 carry 不動人生，你是被掛機的人拖住。

en.content:
I kept dying in raids and assumed I was just bad at the game.
New teammate, new perspective — turns out the old one had been throwing the whole time, and refusing to log off.
Solo run? Cleared it.
en.editorNote: Sometimes you weren't underleveled — your teammate was. For the friend who keeps thinking 'maybe it's me' — you're fine, the party setup was wrong.
variations: The boss I couldn't beat with him? Soloed it. Turns out he was the boss.
You weren't failing to carry your life. You were dragging an AFK player.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'choose peace over love that hurts' empowerment frame; rewritten with 飛航模式 / 通知 Taiwan-friendly phone metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我沒有封鎖他，我只是把人生切到飛航模式。
誰的訊息都進不來，包括我那些「要不要再給他一次機會」的念頭。
zh-tw.editorNote: 釋懷不一定是激烈刪除，有時候是溫柔關機。傳給那個還在跟自己內心拉扯的朋友——飛航模式比封鎖更療癒，因為你關掉的是雜訊，不是回憶。
variations: 通知關掉之後，世界沒變安靜，是我變安靜了。
飛航模式不是逃避，是讓自己終於能聽見自己的聲音。

en.content:
I didn't block him. I just switched my life to airplane mode.
No messages get through — including the 'should I give him one more chance' thoughts in my own head.
en.editorNote: Letting go doesn't have to be a dramatic delete. Sometimes it's a quiet power-down. For the friend still arguing with herself — airplane mode heals deeper than blocking, because what you mute is the noise, not the memories.
variations: After I muted notifications, the world didn't get quieter. I did.
Airplane mode isn't avoidance. It's finally being able to hear yourself.

items.sourceNote: Original riff on 'deleting you like an unwanted app' meme; rewritten with 手機儲存空間 / 釋放空間 storage metaphor

zh-tw.content:
刪掉他之後我手機跳出通知：
「已釋放 87% 儲存空間。」
才知道原來他佔了我這麼多記憶體，難怪每天都當機。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最爽的不是流眼淚，是發現自己變順暢了。傳給那個剛清乾淨對話框的朋友——你不是失去他，是回收了一大塊容量。
variations: 原來不是我手機慢，是他在背景一直耗電。
刪除他不是失戀儀式，是必要的系統維護。

en.content:
After I deleted him, my phone popped up: 
"Freed 87% of storage."
No wonder I was lagging — turns out he'd been hogging the memory the whole time.
en.editorNote: The best post-breakup feeling isn't crying it out — it's noticing you're running smoother. For the friend who just cleared the chat thread: you didn't lose him, you reclaimed a huge chunk of bandwidth.
variations: My phone wasn't slow. He was just draining the battery in the background.
Deleting him wasn't a breakup ritual. It was overdue system maintenance.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '失戀是去找真正幸福的路上' framing; rewritten with 過站 / 終點站 MRT metaphor for Taiwan readers

zh-tw.content:
他不是你的終點站，他只是你誤搭的那班車。
下車的時候你以為迷路了，其實只是還沒走到對的月台。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那個還在問「我是不是錯過真愛了」的朋友。失戀不是走錯路，是走錯車，下一班會來。
variations: 你不是失去他，你只是還沒到站。
錯的人是過站，對的人才是終點。

en.content:
He wasn't your destination — he was just the wrong train.
Getting off felt like being lost, but really you just hadn't reached the right platform yet.
en.editorNote: For the friend asking 'did I miss my one true love?' A breakup isn't taking the wrong path — it's taking the wrong train. The next one is coming.
variations: You didn't lose him. You just hadn't arrived yet.
The wrong person is a stop. The right one is the destination.

items.sourceNote: Original riff on 'lost 180 pounds of dead weight' breakup gym joke; rewritten with 健身房 / 體脂 Taiwan-friendly metaphor

zh-tw.content:
分手後我量體脂，數字沒變。
但心裡那塊「他」的脂肪掉了 65 公斤，難怪走路都變輕了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 分手最有效的減重不是斷食，是斷聯。傳給那個剛甩掉爛桃花的朋友——體重沒輕，但靈魂瘦了一圈。
variations: 我沒瘦，是少背了一個人的人生。
分手是最強有氧，主要燃燒的是情緒脂肪。

en.content:
Stepped on the scale after the breakup. Same number.
But the 65 kg of "him" weighing down my soul? Gone. No wonder I walk lighter now.
en.editorNote: The most effective post-breakup weight loss isn't fasting — it's going no-contact. For the friend who just dropped a toxic ex: the scale didn't change, but your soul got lean.
variations: I didn't lose weight. I just stopped carrying someone else's life.
Breakups are the best cardio. Mostly emotional fat burned.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'this is where my glow up begins' healing IG caption trend; rewritten as a 起床 / 鏡子 morning routine reflection

zh-tw.content:
分手後第三十天，我終於可以在鏡子前停留超過三秒。
看著自己沒哭的臉，覺得這個人比我想像中還要值得被愛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀的療癒不是大徹大悟，是某天早上突然不想再迴避鏡子。傳給那個慢慢長出自己的朋友——你不是變了，你是回來了。
variations: 我沒有變得更好，我只是終於認得鏡子裡的自己。
原來愛自己的第一步，是願意看自己超過三秒鐘。

en.content:
Day thirty after the breakup. I finally stood in front of the mirror for more than three seconds.
Looking at a face that wasn't crying, I realized — this person is more worth loving than I'd remembered.
en.editorNote: Healing isn't some big epiphany — it's the morning you stop avoiding the mirror. For the friend slowly growing back into herself: you didn't change, you came home.
variations: I didn't become better. I just finally recognized the person in the mirror.
Turns out the first step of self-love is being willing to look at yourself for three full seconds.

items.sourceNote: Original riff on savage breakup captions; rewritten with 雲端備份 / 雞肋檔案 cloud storage metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我沒有刪掉他的回憶，我只是把它丟進雲端深處。
反正打開要花三十秒，沒事誰會去翻雞肋檔案。
zh-tw.editorNote: 釋懷不一定是燒光，有時候是歸檔。傳給那個還沒辦法狠下心刪除的朋友——丟進雲端就好，你已經很久沒手動同步了。
variations: 他不是被刪除，是被歸檔。差別在於我再也沒空打開。
回憶不是垃圾，是冷資料。需要的時候再說，不需要就讓它睡著。

en.content:
I didn't delete the memories of him. I just dumped them into the deepest folder in the cloud.
It takes 30 seconds to open — and who actually digs through dead files for fun?
en.editorNote: Letting go doesn't have to be a bonfire. Sometimes it's just archiving. For the friend who can't bring herself to hit delete: drop it in the cloud. You stopped syncing manually a long time ago.
variations: He wasn't deleted. He was archived. Difference is, I never have time to open it anymore.
Memories aren't trash. They're cold storage. Pull them if needed, otherwise let them sleep.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '放下不代表你輸了，是你選擇更愛自己' frame; rewritten with 比賽 / 棄賽 sports/game metaphor

zh-tw.content:
離開他不是我認輸，是我終於發現這場比賽根本沒有獎品。
我選擇下場去找一場，會讓我變更強的人生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 分手不是棄賽，是換場。傳給那個還在覺得「我是不是先放手就輸了」的朋友——根本沒有人在計分，是你自己一直在熬。
variations: 我沒輸給他，我只是離開了一場沒有冠軍的比賽。
棄賽不是失敗，是承認這場局根本不值得你拼命。

en.content:
Leaving him wasn't me losing. It was me finally realizing this game had no prize.
I chose to walk off the field and find a life that actually makes me stronger.
en.editorNote: A breakup isn't quitting — it's switching arenas. For the friend who still thinks 'whoever lets go first loses': there's no scoreboard. You've just been grinding alone.
variations: I didn't lose to him. I just walked out of a match with no trophy.
Quitting isn't failure. It's admitting the game wasn't worth your blood.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'healing is not linear' modern therapy-speak quote; rewritten with 走樓梯 / 跌一階 staircase metaphor

zh-tw.content:
療癒不是一條直線，是一座有時要回頭走的樓梯。
今天跌一階沒關係，明天你還是比昨天高一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那個剛走出來、又突然在便利商店哭出來的朋友。退步不是失敗，是身體還記得，但你已經在往前走了。
variations: 走得慢沒關係，重點是你沒有走回去。
倒退一步不是輸，是身體在提醒你它還沒準備好。

en.content:
Healing isn't a straight line. It's a staircase you sometimes have to walk backwards on.
Slipping a step today is fine — you're still higher than yesterday.
en.editorNote: For the friend who finally felt okay, then burst into tears in a convenience store. Going backwards isn't failure — your body just remembers. You're still moving forward.
variations: Going slow is fine. The point is you didn't go back.
A step backwards isn't losing. It's your body saying it wasn't quite ready yet.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'closure is a decision you make within yourself' frame; rewritten with 等對方道歉 / 自己關門 wait-for-apology metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我不再等他來給我一個交代了。
門是我自己關的，鑰匙也由我來收好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在等前任道歉的朋友。等不到的交代，就由自己給。你不是原諒他，是放過自己。
variations: 結束一段關係不需要對方簽名，只需要你自己點頭。
他沒給的句點，我自己畫上去了。

en.content:
I stopped waiting for him to give me closure.
I'm the one who closed the door. I'm the one who's keeping the key.
en.editorNote: For the friend still waiting on an apology that will never come. The closure you're owed, you can give yourself. You're not forgiving him — you're letting yourself off the hook.
variations: Ending a relationship doesn't need his signature. It just needs your nod.
He never gave me a period at the end of the sentence. So I wrote one in myself.

items.sourceNote: Original riff on the 不再打擾 motif common in TW breakup listicles; rewritten with 已讀不回 / 自己已讀 read-receipt metaphor

zh-tw.content:
以前最怕他已讀不回，現在我自己把對話已讀，然後關掉。
原來最後贏的，是那個先點開、又最快放下的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最大的轉折，是從追著看訊息，變成滑過就算了。傳給那個終於對通知列免疫的朋友——你不是無情，你只是把心拿回來自己用了。
variations: 已讀不回最強的版本，是我已讀了，但不再覺得需要回應。
他訊息還在，我已經不在那個會難過的人裡了。

en.content:
I used to dread him leaving me on read. Now I read it myself — and just close the app.
Turns out whoever opens it first and lets go fastest is the one who wins.
en.editorNote: The biggest turning point after a breakup is going from refreshing the chat to swiping past it. For the friend who's finally numb to the notification bar — you're not heartless, you just took your heart back for yourself.
variations: The ultimate 'left on read' is when I read it and just don't feel like replying anymore.
His message is still there. I'm just not the version of me that hurts about it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you are worthy of love' self-worth quote genre; rewritten with 菜單 / 不點他了 restaurant menu metaphor

zh-tw.content:
人生這份菜單我重新看了一次，
他不是不好吃，是我已經吃膩了，而且我發現我值得更新鮮的東西。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在懷疑「是不是我太挑」的朋友。不是他不夠好，是你長大了。長大的胃，要吃新的東西。
variations: 他不是被淘汰，是從我的菜單上下架了。
你不是難搞，你只是終於知道自己想吃什麼。

en.content:
I read the menu of my life over again.
He wasn't bad — I just got tired of the dish, and I realized I deserve something fresher.
en.editorNote: For the friend still wondering 'am I being too picky?' He wasn't lacking — you just grew up. A grown-up appetite asks for new things.
variations: He wasn't kicked out. He was just taken off my menu.
You're not high-maintenance. You finally just know what you actually want to eat.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'release what no longer chooses you' theme; rewritten with 房間鑰匙 metaphor about reclaiming personal space

zh-tw.content:
我把那把備用鑰匙從鑰匙圈拿下來了。
不是恨他，是我終於想起來，這個家是我的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種卡在「他還會回來嗎」的階段。把鑰匙拿下來不是報復，是宣告：我的人生不再為他保留位置。
variations: 他沒有再回來過了，但我發現我的家變得更乾淨。
拿掉鑰匙的那刻，我才知道這個家從來都是我一個人撐起來的。

en.content:
I took the spare key off my keychain.
Not out of hate — I just finally remembered that this home is mine.
en.editorNote: For the stage where you're still wondering 'will he come back?' Taking the key off isn't revenge — it's a quiet announcement that your life no longer saves a seat for him.
variations: He never came back. But I noticed my place got a lot cleaner.
The moment I unhooked that key, I realized I'd been holding this home up alone the whole time.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you are enough without them' empowerment line; rewritten with 半個我 / 完整 framing to flip the codependency narrative

zh-tw.content:
我以前以為，失去他我就只剩一半。
後來才發現，原來那一半是他借走的我，現在我只是把自己拿回來了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 獻給愛情裡總是「奉獻自己」奉獻到忘記自己長怎樣的朋友。你不是缺了一塊，你只是終於把外借的部分收回家。
variations: 我不是不完整，我只是花了很久才把借出去的自己領回來。
原來分手不是失去一半，是拿回全部。

en.content:
I used to think losing him meant I was only half of me.
Then I realized — that half was the part of me he borrowed. I just took myself back.
en.editorNote: For the friend who poured so much into love that they forgot what they looked like. You're not missing a piece — you're just collecting the parts you loaned out.
variations: I'm not incomplete. It just took me a while to call back the pieces of me I'd lent out.
Turns out a breakup isn't losing half. It's getting all of yourself back.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '用昨天的孤單，餵養今天的勇敢' format; rewritten with 哭過的枕頭 / 浴巾 specific tactile imagery

zh-tw.content:
那條哭濕的枕頭套，我洗了五次才晾乾。
但下次再哭，我會自己遞衛生紙給自己——不再等任何人說沒事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合剛過完最痛那一週、開始學會自我安慰的人。長大就是發現：可以哭，但不必再期待誰來幫你擦眼淚。
variations: 我還是會哭，但我已經會自己泡熱牛奶了。
下次心碎，我自己抱自己。練過很多次了。

en.content:
That pillowcase I cried into took five washes to dry.
But next time I cry, I'll hand myself the tissues — I'm done waiting for someone to tell me it's okay.
en.editorNote: For the friend just past the worst week, learning to comfort themselves. Growing up means: you can still cry, you just stop waiting for someone else to wipe the tears.
variations: I still cry sometimes. But now I make my own warm milk afterward.
Next heartbreak, I'll hold myself. I've had plenty of practice.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '分手快樂' theme; rewritten with 紅綠燈 / 一直等他 traffic metaphor about waiting for someone who's not coming

zh-tw.content:
我等他像在等一個壞掉的紅綠燈，
後來才知道，那個路口根本沒人會來，我可以直接走了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在每天滑訊息、等對方主動的朋友。他不是還沒來，是他根本不會來。綠燈是你自己給自己的。
variations: 原來我不是在等他，是不敢承認他不會出現。
壞掉的不是紅綠燈，是我堅持不轉彎的腳。

en.content:
I waited for him like I was waiting at a broken traffic light.
Then I realized — no one's coming to that intersection. I can just walk.
en.editorNote: For the friend still checking their phone every hour, waiting for him to reach out first. He isn't late — he isn't coming. The green light is the one you give yourself.
variations: I wasn't really waiting for him. I was just scared to admit he wasn't coming.
The light wasn't broken. I just refused to turn around.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'rediscovering yourself' theme; rewritten with 房間重新裝潢 metaphor for emotional reorganization

zh-tw.content:
分手之後我把房間重新擺過一次。
床換邊、窗簾換色、丟掉他送的那個馬克杯——
我才發現，原來我可以決定的事，比我以為的多很多。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給開始想「下一步怎麼辦」的人。從一個小角落開始改變吧。重新擺一張桌子、換一盞燈，你會發現生活的主導權一直在你手上。
variations: 從換床單那一刻起，我就不是同一個人了。
他不在了，但我的房間第一次完全屬於我。

en.content:
After the breakup, I rearranged my whole room.
Moved the bed, swapped the curtains, threw out the mug he gave me —
and realized how many things I'd been allowed to decide all along.
en.editorNote: For the friend just starting to ask 'what now?' Start with one corner. Move a desk, swap a lamp. You'll remember the steering wheel was always in your hands.
variations: I haven't been the same person since the day I changed the sheets.
He's gone, and for the first time my room is fully mine.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'tune out the noise / find your own frequency' empowerment frame; rewritten with 收音機 / 雜訊 radio metaphor about finally stopping the search

zh-tw.content:
我以前把整台收音機轉爛，就為了找到他那一台。
後來才發現，雜訊聽久了會以為是音樂——
我關掉那台收音機，整個房間才終於安靜下來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在分析他每一條限動、每一個已讀時間的朋友。你不是在追蹤他，你只是習慣了背景音。關掉的那一刻，你會聽見自己的呼吸。
variations: 我不是收不到他的訊號了，是我終於不想再調頻了。
原來那不是愛情，是我自己腦補出來的雜訊。

en.content:
I used to spin the dial on the whole radio, just to find his frequency.
Then I realized — listen to static long enough and you start calling it music.
I switched the radio off, and the whole room finally went quiet.
en.editorNote: For the friend still analyzing his every story, every read receipt timestamp. You're not stalking him — you just got used to the background noise. The moment you turn it off, you'll hear yourself breathe again.
variations: It's not that I lost his signal. I just stopped wanting to tune in.
Turns out that wasn't love. It was static I'd convinced myself was a song.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'closing the chapter / new beginnings' empowerment frame; rewritten with 跨年倒數 / 新的一年 New Year countdown metaphor about choosing when your year actually ends

zh-tw.content:
我決定今天就是我自己的跨年。
哭過的那一年到此為止，凌晨零點之後，
我不欠他任何一句「新年快樂」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那個覺得「等到某個日期才能重新開始」的朋友。新年是你說了算。今天就是你的一月一號，今晚就是你的倒數。不需要許願，就許自己。
variations: 倒數五四三二一——他從我的時區裡正式離線。
我把他留在去年了。今天起，是我自己的新元年。

en.content:
I've decided today is my New Year's Eve.
The year I spent crying ends right here. At midnight,
I don't owe him a 'happy new year.'
en.editorNote: For the friend waiting for 'the right date' to start over. You set the calendar. Today is your January 1st. Tonight is your countdown. Skip the wish — just bet on yourself.
variations: Five, four, three, two, one — he's officially offline in my timezone.
I left him in last year. Starting today, it's my own year one.

items.sourceNote: Original riff on the '對的人' Taiwan breakup motif; rewritten with 拼圖 / 別人的盒子 jigsaw puzzle metaphor about being the wrong puzzle entirely

zh-tw.content:
我以為我們是少了一塊的拼圖，
後來才看清——他是別人盒子裡的，從一開始就不是我這幅圖的零件。
我不是缺一塊，是我把錯的那塊硬塞進來了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合還在問「我們到底差在哪」的朋友。答案是——根本不是同一盒。硬塞進去的那塊，再用力都不會貼合。把他放回他的盒子，你的圖才看得出來。
variations: 他不是我少的那一塊，是我撿錯盒子的證據。
強行湊上去的拼圖，邊角總是會翹起來。

en.content:
I thought we were a puzzle missing one piece.
Turns out — he was from someone else's box. He was never part of my picture to begin with.
I wasn't incomplete. I was just forcing the wrong piece in.
en.editorNote: For the friend still asking 'what was missing between us?' The answer: you weren't even the same puzzle. Force a wrong piece in and the edges always lift. Put him back in his own box — that's when your picture comes through.
variations: He wasn't my missing piece. He was proof I'd grabbed the wrong box.
Pieces you force in always pop back up at the corners.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '學會自己走過風雨' classic TW breakup theme; rewritten with 雨傘 / 自己撐 umbrella metaphor about learning to weather your own storms

zh-tw.content:
以前下雨我都等他來接，後來我學會自己帶傘。
再後來我發現——
就算淋濕了，回家也只有我一個人會擔心我，
那從一開始就應該是我自己的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛開始一個人面對下雨天的朋友。不是沒人愛你，是你發現只有自己會永遠在那裡。學會自己撐傘，不是寂寞，是長大。
variations: 我不再期待誰來接我，因為我已經學會看天氣預報了。
傘是我自己的，路也是。原來我一直比想像中更能走。

en.content:
I used to wait for him to pick me up when it rained. Then I learned to carry my own umbrella.
Later I figured out —
even if I got soaked, the only person at home worried about me was me.
It was always supposed to be my job.
en.editorNote: For the friend just learning to face rainy days alone. It's not that no one loves you — you just realized you're the only one who'll always be there. Holding your own umbrella isn't loneliness. It's growing up.
variations: I stopped waiting to be picked up. I just started checking the forecast.
The umbrella is mine. The road is mine. Turns out I could always walk further than I thought.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'choose healing over history' theme common in 2026 breakup listicles; rewritten with 翻舊照 / 滑相簿 phone-album metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我刪了相簿，不是怕想起他，
是發現我每次點進去，都在找一個已經不存在的版本的我們。
那個版本，就連當時都不是真的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那個還會偷偷滑前任相簿的朋友。不是無情，是看清——你懷念的那一幕，從來就不是它看起來的樣子。刪掉的不是回憶，是濾鏡。
variations: 我留著相片不是捨不得他，是捨不得當時還那麼相信的自己。
回憶不需要證據，會留下的本來就不會忘。

en.content:
I deleted the album — not because I was afraid of remembering,
but because every time I opened it, I was looking for a version of us that doesn't exist.
That version wasn't even real back then.
en.editorNote: For the friend still scrolling their ex's photo album at midnight. It's not cold — it's clarity. The moment you miss was never quite what it looked like. You're not deleting memories, you're deleting the filter.
variations: I kept the photos not because I missed him, but because I missed the me who still believed.
Memories don't need receipts. What's meant to stay won't be forgotten.

items.sourceNote: Original riff on the 'one day at a time' healing theme from 2026 English breakup compilations; rewritten with 進度條 / loading bar metaphor

zh-tw.content:
療傷不是進度條，沒有百分之幾的顯示。
有時候你以為快好了，會突然回到 30%。
那不是退步——是因為你終於敢去看清楚還沒處理完的地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那個覺得「都過半年了，怎麼還是會哭」的朋友。療傷沒有截止日，倒退不是失敗，是你還有勇氣面對沒清完的部分。慢慢來，不丟臉。
variations: 心情不是 KPI，不必每天交報告。
好起來不是一條直線，是有上有下的心電圖——能跳才是活著。

en.content:
Healing isn't a loading bar. There's no percentage on the screen.
Sometimes when you think you're almost done, you drop back to 30%.
That's not regression — it's you finally brave enough to look at the parts you skipped.
en.editorNote: For the friend going 'it's been six months, why am I still crying?' Healing has no deadline. Going backwards isn't failure — it means you're brave enough to face what you hadn't unpacked. Take your time. There's no shame in it.
variations: Your feelings aren't a KPI. You don't owe anyone a daily report.
Getting better isn't a straight line. It's a heartbeat — the ups and downs mean you're alive.

items.sourceNote: Original take on 'love yourself first' Taiwan breakup motif; rewritten with 自己接自己 / pick yourself up airport metaphor

zh-tw.content:
他沒來接我，沒關係。
我自己叫了車，自己拖著行李，自己回家洗了澡。
原來我這輩子最可靠的接機，一直都是我自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在等對方來「拯救」她的朋友。不是要逞強，是發現你早就會的事——叫車、拎包、回家——他從來不是必要條件。你才是。
variations: 我以為我在等他，後來才發現是在等自己長大。
最穩的依靠不會遲到，也不會傳「我塞車」——那就是你自己。

en.content:
He didn't show up at the airport. That's fine.
I called my own ride, dragged my own suitcase, went home and showered alone.
Turns out the most reliable pickup in my life was always me.
en.editorNote: For the friend still waiting for someone to come 'rescue' her. This isn't about being tough — it's noticing you've always known how. Calling the ride, grabbing the bag, going home. He was never the requirement. You are.
variations: I thought I was waiting for him. Turns out I was waiting for me to grow up.
The most dependable ride never runs late or texts 'stuck in traffic.' That ride is you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '不適合就放手' theme across Taiwan breakup compilations; rewritten with 退貨 / 不合身衣服 return-the-clothes metaphor

zh-tw.content:
不合身的衣服不是醜，是不適合你。
硬要塞進去只會卡住拉鍊、扯壞縫線。
離開不是承認失敗，是承認尺寸從一開始就不對。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合還在自我懷疑「是不是我不夠好」的朋友。不是你不好，是這件不合身。不合身的關係穿越久越走樣——退掉，去找穿起來像你自己的那一件。
variations: 適合的關係不會讓你每天都在縮小腹。
不是這件衣服爛，是它本來就不是為你做的。

en.content:
Clothes that don't fit aren't ugly — they're just not yours.
Force yourself in and you'll snap the zipper and tear the seam.
Leaving isn't admitting failure. It's admitting the size was wrong from the start.
en.editorNote: For the friend still doubting 'maybe I wasn't enough.' You're not the problem — the fit was. Relationships that don't fit only warp you further the longer you wear them. Return it. Go find one that fits like it was made for you.
variations: The right relationship doesn't make you suck in your stomach every day.
The clothes aren't bad. They were just never made for you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'healing is not linear' theme common in 2026 breakup collections; original 樓梯/電梯 metaphor added

zh-tw.content:
療傷不是搭電梯，是爬樓梯。
有時候你走到五樓，覺得自己終於好了，
隔天又掉回二樓——
沒關係，你還是比一開始高了兩層。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那個以為自己「明明已經好了」卻又突然崩潰的朋友。療癒沒有按下加速鍵的方法，只有今天比昨天高一階的累積。
variations: 復原從來不是一條直線，是一條會回頭的路。
你不是退步了，你只是在休息一下。

en.content:
Healing isn't an elevator. It's a staircase.
Sometimes you reach the fifth floor and think you're finally okay,
then you wake up back on the second.
That's fine — you're still two floors higher than where you started.
en.editorNote: Send this to the friend who thought they were 'fine' and just relapsed into crying. There's no fast-forward button on healing. Only one step higher than yesterday, one day at a time.
variations: Recovery was never a straight line. It's a road that doubles back.
You're not regressing. You're just catching your breath.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '放下不是忘記' theme in Taiwan letting-go quote compilations; original 把鑰匙還回去 metaphor

zh-tw.content:
放下他，不是把他從心裡刪除。
是把他放回他自己的人生，
然後把那串鑰匙從你的口袋裡，輕輕拿出來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合還在問「我要怎麼忘掉他」的朋友。重點不是忘記，是把他從「我的責任範圍」搬出去——他過得好不好，不再是你需要扛的事。
variations: 你不用把他刪掉，只要不再幫他保留位置就好。
他的人生你不用再插手，包括他過得好不好。

en.content:
Letting go isn't deleting them from your heart.
It's putting them back into their own life,
and quietly taking their keys out of your pocket.
en.editorNote: For the friend still asking 'how do I forget them?' You don't have to forget. You just have to stop holding the spare key. Whether they're doing okay is no longer your job to carry.
variations: You don't have to delete them. You just have to stop saving their seat.
Their life isn't yours to manage anymore — including whether they're okay.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you deserve peace without an apology' theme; original 等不到的道歉 / 自己頒發畢業證書 metaphor

zh-tw.content:
他可能永遠不會道歉，
也不會承認他傷害過你。
那張道歉信你等不到了——
所以畢業證書，自己頒給自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那個還在等對方「有一天會懂」的朋友。有些人不會回頭，不會懺悔，不會給你一個閉環。閉環自己給。你已經畢業了，不用等他簽名。
variations: 你不需要他承認傷害你，才能開始好起來。
句點是自己畫上去的，不是等他來蓋章。

en.content:
They might never apologize.
They might never admit they hurt you.
That apology letter isn't coming —
so hand yourself the diploma instead.
en.editorNote: For the friend still waiting for the day the ex 'finally understands.' Some people never come back, never repent, never give you closure. Closure is something you sign for yourself. You've already graduated. You don't need their signature.
variations: You don't need them to admit they hurt you in order to start getting better.
The period at the end of the sentence is yours to draw — not theirs to stamp.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'good things fall apart so better things fall together' (Marilyn Monroe attributed); original 搬家 / 把舊家具丟掉 metaphor

zh-tw.content:
失戀像搬家。
剛開始你會捨不得每一件舊家具，
後來才發現——
新的房子不是少了什麼，是終於放得下你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後的「空」很可怕，但那個空不是缺口，是空間。適合貼給那個剛搬出舊回憶的朋友——新生活不是少了他，是終於有位置給你自己。
variations: 舊家具丟掉那一刻會痛，但新空間會謝謝你。
失去他空出來的位置，本來就是要留給你自己的。

en.content:
A breakup is like moving house.
At first you can't bear to throw out any old furniture.
Later you realize —
the new place isn't missing anything. It finally has room for you.
en.editorNote: The 'emptiness' after a breakup is terrifying, but that empty space isn't a hole — it's room. Send this to the friend who just moved the old memories out. The new life isn't missing them. It finally has space for you.
variations: Throwing out the old furniture hurts in the moment, but the new room will thank you.
The space they left behind was always meant for you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'breakups are redirections, not endings' theme in 2026 healing collections; original GPS / 重新規劃路線 metaphor

zh-tw.content:
分手不是迷路，是 GPS 在重新規劃路線。
你以為的捷徑剛剛被封了，
系統正在幫你找一條真的能到的路。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那個剛剛在哭著問「我接下來要怎麼辦」的朋友。你沒有走丟，只是原本的路被攔起來了。導航沒壞，目的地也沒變，只是會多花一點時間。
variations: 你不是走錯路，是那條路本來就不通。
重新規劃路線不代表回到起點，是繞過剛剛塞住的地方。

en.content:
A breakup isn't getting lost. It's the GPS recalculating.
The shortcut you thought you had just got blocked off.
The system is rerouting you to a road that actually goes somewhere.
en.editorNote: For the friend crying 'what do I do now?' You're not lost — your original road just closed. The GPS still works, the destination hasn't moved. It's just going to take a little longer.
variations: You didn't take the wrong road. That road was always a dead end.
Rerouting doesn't mean going back to the start. It means going around what just blocked you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you are whole, not half' self-love theme; original 一整個披薩 / 不是半塊 metaphor

zh-tw.content:
你不是少了一半的人，
你本來就是一整個披薩。
他不過是當初坐在你旁邊、想分一塊的人——
他走了，披薩還是你的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在說「我感覺自己不完整」的朋友。你不是缺一塊，他從來就不是你的一部分，只是借坐你旁邊的客人。客人走了，你還是滿的。
variations: 你不是失去另一半，是收回了原本就屬於你的位子。
他離開後你沒變小，只是空出了那個本來就不該分出去的位置。

en.content:
You're not someone's missing half.
You were always a whole pizza.
They were just the person who sat next to you and wanted a slice.
They left. The pizza is still yours.
en.editorNote: For the friend still saying 'I feel incomplete.' You're not missing a piece. They were never part of you — just a guest at your table. The guest left. You're still full.
variations: You didn't lose your other half. You just took back the seat that was always yours.
You didn't shrink when they left. You just stopped giving away the slice that wasn't theirs.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'closure comes from within' healing theme; original 把對話視窗關掉 / 不是封鎖 metaphor

zh-tw.content:
你不需要刪掉他、封鎖他、或最後再吵一架。
你只需要把那個對話視窗，
從你心裡的最上面，輕輕往下拉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合還在掙扎要不要徹底斷聯的朋友。放下不是表演，不需要一個盛大的儀式。你只要不再每天打開那個對話視窗，他就會自己滑到最下面。
variations: 你不用刻意忘記他，只要不再讓他排在第一個就好。
真的放下，不是封鎖，是不再置頂。

en.content:
You don't have to delete them, block them, or have one last fight.
You just have to take that chat window
and gently scroll it down from the top of your mind.
en.editorNote: For the friend still wrestling with whether to go full no-contact. Moving on isn't a performance. It doesn't need a grand ceremony. Just stop opening that chat window every day, and it'll slide to the bottom on its own.
variations: You don't have to force yourself to forget them. Just stop pinning them to the top.
Real letting go isn't blocking. It's no longer keeping them at the top of the list.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'loving yourself loudly after heartbreak' empowerment theme; original 重新註冊一個自己 metaphor

zh-tw.content:
分手後最酷的事，
不是換髮型、換工作、換城市，
是發現原來「他喜歡的那個我」可以登出，
而真正的我，從來就沒被刪除過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個分手後在問「我到底是誰」的朋友。你不是失去自己，你只是把那個為了配合他而開的分身帳號登出了。主帳號還在，密碼一直是你的。
variations: 你不需要變成一個新的人，只需要登出那個為了愛他而活的版本。
失戀讓你失去的，從來就不是你自己。

en.content:
The coolest thing after a breakup
isn't a new haircut, a new job, or a new city.
It's realizing the version of you he liked can be logged out,
and the real you was never deleted.
en.editorNote: For the friend asking 'who am I now?' after a breakup. You didn't lose yourself — you just logged out of the side account you opened to match him. The main account is still there. The password was always yours.
variations: You don't have to become a new person. Just log out of the version of you that lived to love him.
Heartbreak never takes away who you actually are.

items.sourceNote: Original take on '不被選擇不代表不夠好' Taiwan breakup motif; rewritten with 書店 / 不是每本書都會被買走 metaphor

zh-tw.content:
你站在書店架上，他沒帶你回家。
那不代表你不好看、不好讀、不夠暢銷。
只代表那天走進來的人，剛好不是你的讀者。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那個還在問「為什麼他不選我」的朋友。不被選不等於不好。書店裡有上萬本好書，不是每本都會被同一個人帶走，但每本都會等到自己的讀者。
variations: 他沒選你，不是你不夠好，是他沒那個眼光。
好書不會因為沒被買走就變爛書，你也一樣。

en.content:
You sat on the bookstore shelf and he didn't take you home.
That doesn't mean you're not beautiful, not well-written, not a bestseller.
It just means the person who walked in that day wasn't your reader.
en.editorNote: For the friend still asking 'why didn't he choose me?' Not being chosen doesn't mean you're not good. A bookstore has thousands of great books — not every reader takes the same one home, but every one of them finds its reader eventually.
variations: He didn't pick you. That's not your fault — it's his taste.
A great book doesn't become a bad book just because it wasn't bought. Neither do you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'accepting the storm has passed' healing motif; original 天氣預報 / 那場雨沒下 weather forecast metaphor about preparing for emotional damage that never arrives

zh-tw.content:
我每天都帶傘出門，就怕想起他的那場雨忽然下下來。
後來才發現，那場雨早就過了，
我只是還沒把濕掉的自己晾乾。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明已經分手好幾個月，卻還在「萬一忽然崩潰」的朋友。雨早就停了，你不需要再隨身帶傘，回家把窗戶打開，讓自己風乾就好。
variations: 我不是還愛他，我只是還沒原諒那個淋過雨的自己。
你以為烏雲還在，其實只是你忘了抬頭看天。

en.content:
I carried an umbrella every day, scared the rain of remembering him would suddenly fall.
Then I realized — that storm passed a long time ago.
I just hadn't dried myself off yet.
en.editorNote: For the friend who broke up months ago but still braces for a sudden meltdown. The rain stopped. You don't need to keep an umbrella on you. Go home, open the windows, let yourself air out.
variations: I'm not still in love with him. I just haven't forgiven the version of me that got soaked.
You think the clouds are still there. You just forgot to look up.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'stop trying to reconnect to what no longer serves you' moving-on theme; original Wi-Fi / 密碼換了 metaphor about a relationship that keeps asking for credentials that no longer work

zh-tw.content:
我一直想連上他那台 Wi-Fi，
密碼錯了五次、十次、一百次。
後來才看清——不是我記錯密碼，是他根本換了路由器。
手機關掉、走出門，外面的訊號好得多。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種還在問「為什麼以前明明可以、現在卻什麼都對不上」的朋友。不是你變笨，是對方早就換掉整個系統。別再輸密碼了，重點是走出去，外面到處都是訊號。
variations: 他的 Wi-Fi 早就沒在發了，是我自己的手機還在背景偷連。
你不是被踢出來的，是這個網路根本不存在了。

en.content:
I kept trying to log onto his Wi-Fi.
Wrong password five times, ten times, a hundred.
Then I saw it — I hadn't forgotten the password. He'd swapped out the whole router.
Turn off the phone. Walk outside. The signal's so much better out there.
en.editorNote: For the friend still asking 'why doesn't anything connect anymore, when it used to work?' You didn't get worse. He swapped the whole system. Stop entering passwords. Just step outside — there's signal everywhere.
variations: His Wi-Fi stopped broadcasting a long time ago. My phone was just trying to reconnect in the background.
You didn't get kicked off the network. The network doesn't exist anymore.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you don't owe them more of yourself' empowerment frame; original 演唱會安可 / 不欠那一首 concert encore metaphor about refusing to perform love again for someone who already left

zh-tw.content:
他唱完那首歌就走了，連謝幕都沒等。
我卻一個人站在台上，唱了整場安可。
後來我學會——觀眾離場以後，
我不欠任何一個人多唱一首。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在替已經離開的對象「補完」感情的朋友。他不是還會回來看安可，他連散場流程都沒走完。台上的麥克風是你的，從現在開始，你只為留下來的人唱。
variations: 他走了我才發現，原來那場演唱會他只買了半場票。
別再對著空座位謝幕，留下來的觀眾，包括你自己。

en.content:
He left right after his song, didn't even wait for the curtain call.
And I stayed on stage alone, performing a whole encore.
That's when I learned — once the audience walks out,
I don't owe anyone another song.
en.editorNote: For the friend still 'finishing' the relationship for someone who already left. He's not coming back for the encore — he didn't even stay for the bow. The mic on that stage is yours. From now on, you only sing for the ones who stayed.
variations: When he left I realized — he only ever bought a half-show ticket.
Stop bowing to empty seats. The audience that's left includes you.

items.sourceNote: Original take on '有些人只是過客' Taiwan breakup motif; rewritten with 季節限定 / 冬天的外套 seasonal clothing metaphor about people who were never meant to stay year-round

zh-tw.content:
他像那件你只在冬天穿的外套。
春天來了，你卻還捨不得收進櫃子，
硬把它穿進五月的太陽底下——難怪你會中暑。
收起來，不是丟掉，是承認他只屬於那個季節。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合還在硬撐「我們明明很合」的朋友。有些人就是季節限定，不是不好，是過了那段時間就不合穿了。把他收進櫃子最深處，不是恨，是讓自己換上適合這個季節的衣服。
variations: 他是冬天的人，你卻想穿著他過夏天。
收起一件不合季節的外套，不代表你不喜歡它，只代表你願意過得舒服一點。

en.content:
He was like that coat you only wore in winter.
Spring came, and you couldn't put it away,
so you dragged it into the May sunshine — no wonder you got heatstroke.
Folding it up isn't throwing it out. It's admitting he only belonged to that season.
en.editorNote: For the friend still insisting 'but we were so right together.' Some people are seasonal — not bad, just out of season. Fold him into the back of the closet. It's not hate. It's letting yourself wear something that actually fits the weather.
variations: He was a winter person. You kept trying to wear him through summer.
Putting away an out-of-season coat doesn't mean you didn't love it. It means you'd rather be comfortable.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you can't start the next chapter if you keep re-reading the last one' moving-on theme; rewritten with original 書籤 / 翻頁 bookmark-versus-reading metaphor about being stuck on the same page

zh-tw.content:
你不是放不下，你只是一直停在同一頁。
書籤不是讓你重看，是提醒你下次從這裡繼續。
翻過去，後面還有兩百頁沒寫完。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那個每天回想同一個畫面、同一句話的朋友。卡住不是因為他太重要，是因為你忘了書還有下一頁。書籤的功能是「繼續」，不是「重播」。
variations: 你不是忘不了他，你是還沒翻頁。
故事還沒寫完，別在第三章就闔上書。

en.content:
You're not stuck on him. You're stuck on the same page.
A bookmark isn't for re-reading — it's there so you know where to pick up next.
Flip the page. There's two hundred more left to write.
en.editorNote: For the friend replaying the same scene, the same sentence, every single day. You're not stuck because he mattered too much — you're stuck because you forgot the book keeps going. A bookmark says 'continue,' not 'replay.'
variations: You haven't failed to forget him. You just haven't turned the page.
The story isn't over. Don't close the book on chapter three.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'your value doesn't decrease based on someone's inability to see your worth' self-worth frame; rewritten with original 美術館 / 沒看懂的畫 museum-and-painting metaphor about being misread by the wrong audience

zh-tw.content:
你是一幅美術館的畫，他只是個剛好走錯展區的人。
他沒看懂不是你不夠好，是他根本不是來看你的。
別追到出口去解釋，下一個進來的人，會在你面前站很久。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那個還在反覆問「他為什麼不珍惜我」的朋友。不是你不夠好，是他從頭到尾都沒打算讀你。畫不會追觀眾，畫只負責繼續被掛在那裡，等懂的人來。
variations: 他不懂你不是你的問題，是他買錯票了。
別追著解釋，會懂你的人不需要你說明書。

en.content:
You're a painting in a museum. He just wandered into the wrong wing.
He didn't get you — that's not on you. He wasn't there to look at you in the first place.
Don't chase him to the exit to explain. The next person through that door will stand in front of you for a long time.
en.editorNote: For the friend still asking 'why didn't he see how much I gave?' You weren't lacking. He was never reading you to begin with. Paintings don't chase visitors. They just keep hanging there, waiting for someone who actually came to look.
variations: Him not getting you isn't your problem. He bought the wrong ticket.
Stop chasing people to explain yourself. The right ones don't need a user manual.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '放下，才是最好的不打擾' Taiwan classic breakup motif; rewritten with original 雨傘 / 雨停了 umbrella-after-the-storm metaphor about people who only show up during your hard times

zh-tw.content:
他是你下大雨時借的傘，雨停了你卻不肯還。
撐著一把不屬於你的傘走在大太陽底下，
別人以為你還在淋雨——其實你只是不肯承認，
那場雨早就結束了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明已經沒事、卻還假裝需要對方的朋友。有些感情真的就是「下雨用一下」的功能，雨停了就該還傘。不是他不好，是你早就走出那場雨了，只是還沒放手。
variations: 雨停了還撐傘的人，不是怕雨，是怕承認自己已經好了。
把傘還回去，不是分手，是讓自己重新看見天空。

en.content:
He was an umbrella you borrowed in a downpour, and you never gave it back.
Walking under someone else's umbrella in full sunlight,
people think you're still in the rain — but really, you just won't admit
the storm ended a long time ago.
en.editorNote: Send this to the friend who's actually fine, but keeps pretending she still needs him. Some relationships are just a 'use it during the rain' kind of thing. Storm's over — give the umbrella back. He's not the bad guy. You just walked out of the rain and haven't let go.
variations: People who keep their umbrella up after the rain aren't afraid of getting wet. They're afraid to admit they're fine now.
Giving the umbrella back isn't a breakup. It's letting yourself see the sky again.

items.sourceNote: Original take on '允許自己悲傷' grief-permission motif from Taiwan healing-quote articles; rewritten with 颱風假 / 不上班 typhoon-day-off metaphor giving the heart permission to take a sick day

zh-tw.content:
失戀那幾天，給自己放一個颱風假。
不上班、不營業、不回訊息，
沙發是床、零食是正餐、眼淚是天氣預報。
等風雨過去再開門，沒有人會怪你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那個還在硬撐「我沒事我可以」的朋友。心也是需要請假的。颱風天勉強出門才會出事，給自己一個正當理由停擺幾天，不是脆弱，是基本維修。
variations: 心碎了沒換零件就上班，當然會壞得更徹底。
請假不是逃避，是讓自己有空把碎片掃乾淨。

en.content:
The week you got dumped, give yourself a typhoon day.
No work, no replying, no being a functioning human.
The couch is your bed, snacks are dinner, your tears are the weather forecast.
When the storm passes, you'll open the door — and nobody's going to blame you.
en.editorNote: For the friend still insisting 'I'm fine, I can handle it.' Your heart is allowed to call in sick. Going outside in a typhoon is how people get hurt. Giving yourself a legit reason to shut down for a few days isn't weakness — it's basic maintenance.
variations: Going to work with a broken heart and no spare parts? Of course it breaks all the way.
Taking the day off isn't running away. It's giving yourself time to sweep up the pieces.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'your worth is not negotiable / your value doesn't decrease' self-respect motif; rewritten with a 標價 / price-tag metaphor specific to night-market bargaining culture

zh-tw.content:
你不是夜市攤位上的衣服，
不會因為他沒挑你，就要打折促銷。
他的離開只證明一件事：
他付不起你原本的標價。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那個分手後一直覺得自己「是不是條件不夠好」的朋友。被退貨不代表瑕疵品，可能只是顧客眼光有問題。你的價值不需要他蓋章認證。
variations: 他不要你，不是你不夠好，是他拿不出那個價錢。
降價求售的人最後都被當地攤貨，記得守住自己的牌價。

en.content:
You're not a clearance rack at the night market.
Just because he didn't pick you up doesn't mean you need to mark yourself down.
Him leaving only proves one thing:
he couldn't afford your original price.
en.editorNote: For the friend who keeps wondering 'am I just not good enough?' after a breakup. Being returned doesn't make you defective — sometimes the customer just had bad taste. Your worth doesn't need his stamp of approval.
variations: Him not wanting you doesn't mean you're less. It means he couldn't pay the price.
People who keep discounting themselves end up in the bargain bin. Hold your price.

items.sourceNote: Original take on 'healing is not linear' motif from 2026 breakup healing listicles; rewritten with 進度條 / loading-bar metaphor to capture the non-linear nature of grief

zh-tw.content:
療傷不是進度條，
不會今天 30%、明天 50%、後天就跑完。
它比較像 Wi-Fi 訊號，
有時候滿格，有時候突然斷線。
斷線不是退步，是這條路本來就會這樣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個前兩週看起來很 OK、第三週突然崩潰的朋友。失戀沒有時程表，也沒有什麼「應該好了」。陪她把斷線那天過完就好。
variations: 今天難過不代表前面白走，療傷從來就不是直線。
好了又痛，痛了又好，這就是復原的正常版本。

en.content:
Healing isn't a loading bar.
It doesn't go 30% today, 50% tomorrow, done by Friday.
It's more like a Wi-Fi signal —
full bars sometimes, dropped connection out of nowhere.
Dropping isn't backsliding. It's just how this road works.
en.editorNote: Send this to the friend who seemed fine for two weeks and then fell apart in week three. Heartbreak doesn't come with a schedule or a 'you should be over it by now.' Just sit with her through the dropped-connection day.
variations: A bad day doesn't undo the good ones. Healing was never a straight line.
Better, then worse, then better again — that's the normal version of recovery.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '放下不代表你輸了，而是你選擇更愛自己了' from Taiwan letting-go quote collections; rewritten with a 退追蹤 / unfollow modern social-media angle

zh-tw.content:
退追蹤不是不成熟，
是承認你還沒辦法只當朋友看他的限動。
不是封鎖全世界，
是先把那個會讓你失眠的入口關起來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在「該不該退追前任」糾結三個月的朋友。退追蹤不是恨，是自我保護。等哪天你滑到他的動態心跳不會亂，再加回來也不遲。
variations: 看不到，不是逃避，是讓自己有機會慢慢好起來。
退追蹤的那一秒，你不是輸了，是終於選了自己。

en.content:
Unfollowing isn't immature.
It's admitting you can't watch his stories like a normal friend yet.
You're not blocking the whole world —
you're just closing the door that keeps you up at night.
en.editorNote: For the friend who's been debating 'should I unfollow my ex?' for three months. Unfollowing isn't hatred — it's self-protection. If one day his post pops up and your heart doesn't lurch, you can always follow back.
variations: Looking away isn't running away. It's giving yourself room to actually heal.
The second you hit unfollow, you didn't lose. You finally chose yourself.

items.sourceNote: Original take on '失戀並不代表失去一切 / 重新迎接新的開始' rebirth motif common across Taiwan healing-quote articles; rewritten with a 手機重開機 / phone-reboot metaphor

zh-tw.content:
失戀就像手機卡頓到不行，被迫重開機。
關掉的那幾秒很黑、很安靜、什麼都做不了，
但開回來之後，跑得比之前還順。
那段空白不是壞掉，是系統在清快取。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個覺得「現在什麼都做不了，是不是廢了」的朋友。發呆、躺平、突然不想出門，都是系統在背景更新。等更新完，你會發現自己變輕了。
variations: 現在什麼都不想做，不是擺爛，是你正在重新開機。
黑屏的那幾秒最重要，跳過了才會跑得不順。

en.content:
Heartbreak is like a phone so frozen it has to reboot.
Those few seconds after shutdown — black, silent, useless —
but when it comes back on, it runs smoother than before.
That blank stretch isn't broken. It's the system clearing cache.
en.editorNote: Send this to the friend who feels 'I can't do anything right now, am I just useless?' Spacing out, lying flat, suddenly not wanting to leave the house — that's the system updating in the background. When it's done, you'll feel lighter.
variations: Not wanting to do anything right now isn't laziness. You're rebooting.
The black screen seconds matter most. Skip them and nothing runs right.

items.sourceNote: Original take on '你的價值不會因為他不要你而改變 / your worth is not negotiable' theme from 2026 breakup-quote collections; rewritten with a 退貨 / return-policy metaphor

zh-tw.content:
他退貨你，不代表你有瑕疵。
只代表他不是那個會用你的人。
你不是商品，沒有保固期，
更不用因為他不要，就懷疑自己值不值得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個分手後一直想「是不是我不夠好」的朋友。對方不要你，不是品質問題，是匹配問題。把自我價值的決定權，從前任手裡拿回來。
variations: 他不選你，是他的尺寸不合，不是你的形狀有錯。
你的價值不是由願不願意留下的人決定的。

en.content:
Them returning you doesn't mean you're defective.
It just means they weren't the person who'd use you.
You're not a product. There's no warranty.
And you don't need their 'no' to doubt whether you're enough.
en.editorNote: Send this to the friend who keeps asking 'was I not good enough?' after a breakup. Them not wanting you isn't a quality issue — it's a fit issue. Take the right to decide your own worth back from your ex.
variations: They didn't pick you because the size was wrong for them, not because your shape was wrong.
Your value isn't decided by the people who chose not to stay.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'saying goodbye to a version of love that no longer serves you' motif from 2026 goodbye-breakup listicles; rewritten with a 換季衣櫃 / wardrobe-season metaphor

zh-tw.content:
跟他說再見，不是把愛丟掉，
是把已經穿不下的那件外套收起來。
它陪你過了一個冬天，
但不代表下一個冬天，還會剛好合身。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合給那個一直覺得「分手就是浪費了那幾年」的朋友。那段感情不是失敗，是它本來就只屬於那個版本的你。新的你，需要的不是同一件外套。
variations: 結束一段關係，不是否定它，是承認你已經長大了。
穿不下不是衣服的錯，是你已經不是當時的尺寸。

en.content:
Saying goodbye to them isn't throwing love away.
It's folding up the coat that doesn't fit anymore.
It got you through one winter,
but that doesn't mean the next winter calls for the same one.
en.editorNote: For the friend who feels 'those years were just wasted.' That relationship wasn't a failure — it belonged to the version of you you were then. The new you needs a different coat.
variations: Ending it isn't denying it. It's admitting you've outgrown it.
