The coat didn't get worse. You just aren't that size anymore.

items.sourceNote: Original take on 'every heartbreak cracks open a doorway to new beginnings' theme from 2026 breakup healing collections; rewritten with a 房間騰空 / empty-room metaphor

zh-tw.content:
他走了之後，心裡那塊地方是空的。
但空的不是壞事，
那是你終於有位置，放新的東西進來。
以前塞太滿，連自己都擠不進去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個覺得「沒有他，我什麼都不剩」的朋友。空，不是失去，是空間。等你慢慢把自己擺進來，會發現那個位置原本就該是你的。
variations: 他離開騰出來的不是傷口，是一個你可以重新佈置的空間。
心裡空一塊不可怕，可怕的是繼續被不該留的人佔著。

en.content:
After they left, there's an empty spot inside.
But empty isn't bad —
it means you finally have room to put new things in.
Before, it was packed so full even you couldn't fit.
en.editorNote: Send this to the friend who feels 'without them I have nothing left.' Empty isn't loss — it's space. As you slowly place yourself back in, you'll realize that spot was supposed to be yours all along.
variations: What they left behind isn't a wound. It's a room you get to redecorate.
An empty spot inside isn't scary. What's scary is letting the wrong person keep occupying it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'healing quietly without explaining' theme from 2026 IG breakup caption collections; rewritten with a 不發限動 / 安靜長大 angle

zh-tw.content:
我沒有發任何限動，沒有貼任何傷感歌詞，
那不是我裝沒事，是我終於懂——
有些痛不用昭告天下，安靜的長大比較快。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛分手卻不想被全 IG 知道的朋友。不發文不代表沒事，是她選擇用自己的方式消化。陪她安靜就好，不用追問。
variations: 成熟的痛不是不喊，是知道喊出來也沒人能替你扛。
最深的療傷都是無聲的，畢竟你的人生不需要直播。

en.content:
I didn't post a story. I didn't drop a sad lyric.
That's not me pretending I'm fine — it's me finally getting it.
Some pain doesn't need an audience. You grow up faster in silence.
en.editorNote: For the friend who just broke up but doesn't want all of IG to know. No posts doesn't mean she's okay — she's processing her way. Just sit with her quietly. Don't probe.
variations: Grown-up pain doesn't scream. It just knows screaming won't make anyone else carry it.
The deepest healing is silent. Your life was never meant to be a livestream.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'glow up after breakup' 2026 caption trend; rewritten with a 通勤照鏡子 / commute-reflection moment Taiwan-friendly angle

zh-tw.content:
今天通勤的時候，我在捷運車窗看到自己。
沒有特別打扮，但眼神不一樣了——
原來那種「我不需要被誰選」的氣場，是分手送我的禮物。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最強的 glow up 不是變漂亮，是眼神變了。傳給那個剛開始覺得「一個人也可以」的朋友，那不是錯覺，那是真的長出來的東西。
variations: 他離開以後，我才知道原來自信是這種長相。
最好看的妝容是「我不缺你」這四個字寫在臉上。

en.content:
On the train this morning, I caught my reflection in the window.
Nothing fancy on, but the look in my eyes was different —
turns out 'I don't need to be picked' is the energy this breakup gave me.
en.editorNote: The strongest post-breakup glow-up isn't looking prettier — it's the change in your eyes. For the friend just starting to feel 'I'm okay alone.' That's not in her head — it's real, and it just grew in.
variations: After they left, I finally saw what confidence looks like on me.
The best makeup is 'I'm not missing you' written across your face.

items.sourceNote: Original take on 'from us to me, this solo chapter looks really good' theme; rewritten with a 雙人床 / 整張都是我的 daily-life Taiwan metaphor

zh-tw.content:
雙人床第一次整張都是我的，
睡哪一邊都可以，棉被也不用分。
原來「孤單」跟「自由」之間的差別，
只差在你怎麼想而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀第一個月最大的轉變：你發現一個人睡其實滿爽的。傳給那個剛把另一個枕頭收起來的朋友——這不是孤單，是空間還給你了。
variations: 棉被不用搶之後，我才知道自己睡眠品質有多好。
一個人的床不是冷，是終於可以攤開來睡。

en.content:
First time the whole bed is mine.
Any side I want, the whole blanket too.
Turns out the difference between 'lonely' and 'free' —
is just how you decide to frame it.
en.editorNote: Biggest shift in the first month post-breakup: you realize sleeping alone is actually kind of great. For the friend who just put the second pillow away — that's not loneliness, that's your space coming back to you.
variations: Once I wasn't fighting for the blanket, I learned how well I actually sleep.
A bed for one isn't cold. It's finally room to stretch out.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'exchanged my better half for a better peace' savage 2026 caption format; rewritten with a 換掉吵架對象 / 內心安靜 angle

zh-tw.content:
我沒有失去另一半，我換到了一份安靜。
以前的我每天在解釋、討好、猜他的臉色，
現在的我只需要決定中午吃什麼——
這筆交易我覺得很划算。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把分手當成「升級安靜」就一點都不慘了。傳給那種「分手後反而睡得比較好」的朋友，互相確認這是正常的，不是冷血。
variations: 我交換的不是愛情，是少一個人在我腦袋裡吵架。
他不在以後，我才發現原來腦袋這麼安靜這麼好。

en.content:
I didn't lose a partner. I traded up for peace and quiet.
Old me spent every day explaining, pleasing, reading his mood.
New me only has to decide what to eat for lunch —
honestly, great deal.
en.editorNote: Reframing a breakup as 'upgrading to quiet' takes all the sting out. For the friend sleeping better since the split — let her know that's normal, not cold-hearted.
variations: I didn't trade away love. I traded away someone arguing inside my head.
Once he was gone, I realized how nice it is when my brain is finally quiet.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I turned my heartbreak into a reset button' 2026 glow-up caption trend; rewritten with a 手機重開機 / 卡頓 Taiwan-friendly tech metaphor

zh-tw.content:
心碎不是壞掉，是按到了重開機。
以前卡卡的、跑不動的、一直跳通知的，
重啟之後一次清乾淨——
原來我一直背著別人的後台程式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛分手覺得「我整個人都崩了」的朋友。沒有崩，只是把佔記憶體的東西關掉了。重開之後你會發現自己跑得多順。
variations: 他不是把我弄壞，他只是逼我重灌一次自己。
心碎是 Ctrl+Alt+Delete，不是 Game Over。

en.content:
Heartbreak isn't a malfunction — it's a forced restart.
Everything that was lagging, freezing, pinging you nonstop —
all cleared in one boot.
Turns out you'd been running someone else's background apps the whole time.
en.editorNote: For the friend who just split and feels 'I'm completely broken.' She's not broken — she just closed the apps eating her memory. After the reboot, she'll see how smooth she runs.
variations: He didn't break me. He just forced me to reinstall myself.
Heartbreak is Ctrl+Alt+Delete, not Game Over.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '放下不是丟掉，是把曾經好好放在記憶裡' classic let-go quote; rewritten with a 抽屜深處 / 不上鎖 storage metaphor

zh-tw.content:
放下不是把他從記憶裡刪掉，
是把那段關係收進抽屜最深的地方。
沒有上鎖，但也不會再打開——
因為現在的我，已經不住在那個房間了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 成熟的釋懷不是恨，也不是忘記，是「我知道它在那裡，但我不需要再回去」。傳給那個怕「不再想他就是背叛回憶」的朋友。
variations: 我沒有忘記他，我只是不再需要記得他。
釋懷是把回憶搬進儲藏室，房間騰出來給自己住。

en.content:
Letting go isn't deleting them from your memory.
It's tucking that relationship into the deepest drawer.
Not locked — but I won't open it again either,
because the me of today doesn't live in that room anymore.
en.editorNote: Mature closure isn't hate or amnesia — it's 'I know it's there, I just don't need to go back.' For the friend afraid that not thinking of him is somehow a betrayal of the memory.
variations: I didn't forget him. I just don't need to remember him anymore.
Moving on is putting the memories in storage and turning the bedroom over to yourself.

items.sourceNote: Original riff on 'sometimes the love story you deserve is the one you write with yourself' empowerment frame; rewritten with a 早午餐 / 自己約自己 daily Taiwan ritual

zh-tw.content:
我今天約自己吃早午餐。
不是因為沒人約，是因為我終於知道——
那個願意每週五點起床準備我喜歡的味道的人，
本來就只有我自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最浪漫的轉變：你開始用對待戀人的方式對待自己。傳給那個正在「重新學會單身怎麼好玩」的朋友。
variations: 等別人來愛我之前，我先把自己當喜歡的人對待。
一個人吃早午餐不是寂寞，是我終於配得上好好吃一頓。

en.content:
I took myself out to brunch today.
Not because no one else asked — because I finally got it:
the person willing to get up early to make exactly what I like
was always supposed to be me.
en.editorNote: The most romantic post-breakup shift: you start treating yourself the way you'd treat a partner. For the friend relearning how to enjoy being single.
variations: Before waiting for someone to love me, I started treating myself like someone I love.
Brunch alone isn't lonely. It's me finally believing I deserve a good meal.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'plot twist: the breakup was the best part' savage caption format; rewritten with a 訂閱制 / 自動續約 modern Taiwan-friendly metaphor

zh-tw.content:
他以為分手是他甩了我，
其實是我不續約。
免費試用期看完了，內容沒有比較好，
當然不會升級成年費會員。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把感情當訂閱制看，主導權就回到自己手上了。傳給那個被分手後一直自我懷疑「是不是我不夠好」的朋友——是你不續，不是你被退。
variations: 他不是甩了我，他只是被我從自動續約名單裡刪掉。
試用期結束之後，沒有按續訂的人才是清醒的那個。

en.content:
He thinks the breakup means he dumped me.
No — I just didn't renew.
The free trial ran out, the content wasn't worth it,
so obviously I didn't upgrade to the annual plan.
en.editorNote: Reframing love as a subscription puts the power back in your hands. For the friend stuck on 'maybe I wasn't good enough' — you didn't get cancelled, you cancelled.
variations: He didn't dump me. I just took him off auto-renew.
When the trial ends, the one who doesn't click renew is the one thinking clearly.

items.sourceNote: Original riff on the 'I exited the group chat' savage caption frame; rewritten with a 群組 / 退出 / 通知靜音 messaging-app metaphor for Taiwan readers

zh-tw.content:
我不是被分手，我只是默默退出了那個群組。
沒有公告、沒有解釋，就像那些已讀我訊息三天的人一樣。
退出之後才發現——原來那個群，只有我一個人在認真聊天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把分手當成「退出群組」就沒那麼悲情了。傳給那個一直覺得「是不是我太黏」的朋友——不是你太熱情，是對方早就把通知關靜音了。
variations: 我沒有被踢出群，我是自己按退出的，差很多。
終於退出那個只有我在打字的群組，世界安靜得很舒服。

en.content:
I didn't get dumped. I just quietly left the group chat.
No announcement, no explanation — same energy as the people who leave you on read for three days.
After I left, I realized I was the only one actually talking in there anyway.
en.editorNote: Reframing a breakup as 'leaving the group chat' takes the drama out. For the friend who keeps thinking 'was I too clingy?' — you weren't too much, he just had you muted the whole time.
variations: I didn't get removed from the chat. I hit leave myself. Big difference.
Finally left the group where I was the only one typing. The silence is honestly lovely.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '放下不是遺忘，是把篇章好好闔上' classic let-go theme; rewritten with a 電影散場 / 留到片尾 cinema metaphor

zh-tw.content:
他先走了，我卻一個人留到片尾燈亮。
後來我懂了——不是每場電影都要等到工作人員名單跑完。
好看的我記得就好，散場了就該起身回家。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那個分手後還守在原地、捨不得離場的朋友。故事結束了不代表你輸了，只是這場放完了。下一場，記得買自己喜歡的位子。
variations: 燈亮了還不走的人，不是深情，是還沒承認電影已經演完了。
我把這場記在心裡，但我已經走出影廳了。

en.content:
He left early, but I stayed alone until the house lights came up.
Then it hit me — not every movie needs you to sit through the end credits.
I'll remember the good parts. When it's over, you stand up and go home.
en.editorNote: For the friend still sitting in the same spot, refusing to leave after the breakup. The story ending doesn't mean you lost — this screening is just done. Next time, pick a seat you actually like.
variations: Staying after the lights come up isn't devotion. It's just not admitting the movie's over.
I'll keep this one in my memory. But I've already walked out of the theater.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I stopped setting alarms for someone who never woke up for me' empowerment frame; rewritten with a 鬧鐘 / 賴床 morning-routine metaphor

zh-tw.content:
以前我設五個鬧鐘，是怕錯過他的早安。
現在我設一個鬧鐘，是為了去做我自己想做的事。
原來最該被叫醒的人，從來都是我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最安靜的成長，是你發現早上醒來的理由變成自己。傳給那個剛開始為自己起床的朋友——不是為了等誰，是為了你想過的那一天。
variations: 我不再為了等他的訊息醒來，我為了今天的計畫醒來。
鬧鐘響的時候，我第一個想到的是我自己，這就是進步。

en.content:
I used to set five alarms so I wouldn't miss his good morning.
Now I set one — to get up and do the things I actually want.
Turns out the person who most deserved waking up for was always me.
en.editorNote: The quietest growth after a breakup is realizing your reason to wake up is yourself now. For the friend who's just started getting up for her own day — not waiting on anyone, just living the day she wants.
variations: I don't wake up waiting for his text anymore. I wake up for today's plans.
When the alarm goes off, the first person I think of is me. That's progress.

items.sourceNote: Original take on the '對的人會留下，錯的人是過程' Taiwan breakup motif; rewritten with a 草稿夾 / 沒寄出的訊息 unsent-drafts metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我的草稿夾裡還躺著三十七則沒寄出的訊息。
以前我以為那是還沒說完的話，後來才懂——
那不是想對他說的，是想對那個還愛著他的自己說的。
我把草稿全刪了，因為那個我，已經先放下了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個手機裡藏著一堆「沒勇氣傳出去」訊息的朋友。那些話其實不是給對方的，是給過去的自己的。刪掉它們，不是失去，是你終於不需要再說服自己。
variations: 沒寄出的訊息不是遺憾，是我那時還沒準備好放手的證據。
刪掉草稿的那一刻，我才發現我早就把要說的話，活成了答案。

en.content:
There are still thirty-seven unsent messages sitting in my drafts.
I used to think they were things I hadn't finished saying. Then I understood —
they weren't meant for him. They were for the version of me that still loved him.
I deleted them all, because that version of me had already let go.
en.editorNote: For the friend hiding a folder of 'never had the nerve to send' messages on her phone. Those words were never really for the other person — they were for your past self. Deleting them isn't losing anything. It's no longer needing to convince yourself.
variations: The unsent messages weren't regret. They were proof I wasn't ready to let go yet.
The moment I deleted the drafts, I realized I'd already lived out the answer I was trying to write.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'my glow-up is your loss' empowerment frame; rewritten with a 限量款下架 / 缺貨 sold-out retail metaphor

zh-tw.content:
你錯過的不是打折商品，是限量款。
現在我直接下架，缺貨中，不補貨。
你慢慢後悔，我這邊已經換季了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在徹底走出來、開始過好日子時放 IG 限動。重點不是嗆對方，是宣告自己有多搶手。傳給那個剛變美、開始發光的閨蜜最對味。
variations: 我不是被退貨，是被秒殺完直接停產。
你猶豫的時候，我已經是別人搶不到的款了。

en.content:
What you passed up wasn't a clearance item — it was a limited edition.
Now I'm off the shelf, sold out, no restock.
Take your time regretting it. I've already moved on to the next season.
en.editorNote: Perfect for an IG story when you've fully moved on and life is good. The point isn't to roast them — it's to announce how in-demand you are. Best sent to the friend who just glowed up.
variations: I didn't get returned. I sold out and got discontinued.
While you were hesitating, I became the piece nobody else can get.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the '謝謝你的離開，讓我學會放下' farewell theme; rewritten with a 退租 / 押金 lease-and-deposit metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我把那段感情當成租來的房子，到期就退租了。
押金沒拿回來沒關係，那叫學費。
下一個地方，我要找的是能住一輩子的，不是月底就想搬的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把失戀當成「換房子」就沒那麼難過。傳給那個還在心疼「投入這麼多卻什麼都沒留下」的朋友——那些不是浪費，是讓你看清下次要找什麼。
variations: 退租不丟臉，硬住在會漏水的地方才委屈了自己。
押金當作學費，至少你學會了下次先看清楚再簽約。

en.content:
I treated that relationship like a rented apartment — when the lease was up, I moved out.
Didn't get the deposit back? Fine, call it tuition.
Next place, I'm looking for somewhere I could live for life, not somewhere I'd want to leave by month's end.
en.editorNote: Treating a breakup like changing apartments takes the sting out. For the friend still aching over 'I put in so much and got nothing back' — it wasn't wasted, it showed you what to look for next time.
variations: There's no shame in moving out. Forcing yourself to stay somewhere that leaks is the real loss.
Count the deposit as tuition. At least you learned to read the lease before signing next time.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'ex means example of what I don't need' wordplay; rewritten with a 錯題本 / 重考 study-mistakes metaphor

zh-tw.content:
前任就是我的錯題本，
標記著「這題下次別再選一樣的答案」。
不是失敗，是訂正——而且這次我終於看懂題目了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把感情失誤當成「訂正錯題」最不傷自尊。傳給那個一直自責「怎麼又選錯人」的朋友——錯題訂正過就不會再錯，重點是你有翻開那本本子。
variations: 他不是我的遺憾，是我的錯題本範例。
同樣的題型再出現，這次我會了。

en.content:
My ex is my mistake notebook,
marked with 'don't pick the same answer here next time.'
It's not a failure — it's a correction. And this time, I finally understood the question.
en.editorNote: Framing a relationship slip-up as 'correcting a wrong answer' keeps your dignity intact. For the friend beating herself up over 'why did I pick wrong again?' — once you correct a mistake, you stop repeating it. The point is you opened the notebook.
variations: He's not my regret. He's the worked example in my mistake notebook.
Same kind of question shows up again? This time I know the answer.

items.sourceNote: Original riff on the 'single and thriving / self-love mode activated' caption trend; rewritten with a 飛航模式 / 重新連線 phone-settings metaphor

zh-tw.content:
分手後我把自己調成飛航模式。
不是要躲，是先斷線，把自己重新連回正確的訊號。
等我再上線的時候，已經不是那個一直等他來電的人了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後給自己一段「不回應任何人」的時間不是逃避，是維修。傳給那個剛分手、需要許可一個人靜一靜的朋友。
variations: 飛航模式不是關機，是讓自己有空連回自己。
斷線一陣子，是為了下次連到對的人。

en.content:
After the breakup, I switched myself to airplane mode.
Not to hide — to disconnect, and reconnect to the right signal.
By the time I came back online, I wasn't the person who kept waiting for his call anymore.
en.editorNote: Giving yourself a stretch of 'not answering to anyone' after a breakup isn't avoidance — it's maintenance. For the friend who just broke up and needs permission to be alone for a while.
variations: Airplane mode isn't powering off. It's making room to reconnect to yourself.
Going offline for a while is how you connect to the right person next time.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the '謝謝你來過我的世界，現在換我學會放下' closure theme; rewritten with a 看完一場電影 / 散場 cinema metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我把你想成一場看完的電影。
當下哭得唏哩嘩啦，散場後燈一亮，我還是得走回自己的生活。
謝謝這部片很好看，但片尾跑完了，我不會一直坐在位子上等續集。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把一段感情當成「一部看完的電影」，能哭、能感謝、也能起身離場。傳給那個遲遲不肯離開戲院、還在等對方回來的朋友。
variations: 好看的電影不一定要有續集，散場了就讓它是回憶。
片尾名單跑完還不走的人，最後只會被工作人員請出場。

en.content:
I think of you as a movie I've already watched.
I sobbed through it, but when the lights came up, I still had to walk back to my own life.
Thanks — it was a good film. But the credits have rolled, and I'm not going to sit in my seat waiting for a sequel.
en.editorNote: Treating a relationship like 'a movie you've finished' lets you cry, give thanks, and still stand up to leave. For the friend lingering in the theater, still waiting for them to come back.
variations: A great movie doesn't need a sequel. When it's over, let it be a memory.
Stay in your seat after the credits and eventually the staff will ask you to leave.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'not heartbroken, just free trial expired' caption format; rewritten with 試用期沒過 / 轉正 workplace metaphor for Taiwan audience

zh-tw.content:
我不是被分手，是試用期沒過，沒幫他轉正。
表現不及格還想領年終，門都沒有。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把感情當求職看，分手就變成「人資的理性決定」。適合在分手後第一次跟同事吃飯時自嘲，氣氛瞬間輕鬆。
variations: 他履歷寫得很漂亮，可惜實作能力跟面試時差很多。
感情也有試用期，不適任就不續約，這不是無情，是專業。

en.content:
I didn't get dumped. He just failed his probation, so I declined to make him permanent.
Underperforming and still expecting a year-end bonus? Not a chance.
en.editorNote: Treating love like hiring turns a breakup into 'a rational HR decision.' Great for self-deprecating laughs at your first dinner with coworkers post-split.
variations: His resume looked great. Shame the actual performance didn't match the interview.
Relationships have a probation period too. Not renewing an underperformer isn't cruelty — it's professionalism.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'healing isn't linear' theme; rewritten with 退潮 / 海岸線 tide metaphor

zh-tw.content:
復原不是一條直線，是潮水。
今天退得乾乾淨淨，明天又漲回來打濕你的腳。
別罵自己退步，海從來就是這樣，最後它還是會退。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀復原最大的挫折是「明明好了，怎麼又難過」。傳給那個責怪自己「怎麼還沒走出來」的朋友——你沒退步，這就是海的樣子。
variations: 好了又痛，痛了又好，這不是原地踏步，是潮汐。
今天哭了不代表前面白走，浪退過的沙灘還是比昨天乾。

en.content:
Healing isn't a straight line. It's the tide.
Today it pulls all the way out, and tomorrow it rushes back to soak your feet.
Don't scold yourself for sliding back — the sea has always worked this way, and it always goes out again.
en.editorNote: The hardest part of recovery is 'I thought I was fine, why does it hurt again?' For the friend blaming herself for 'not being over it yet' — you didn't regress, this is just what the tide does.
variations: Better, then aching, then better again — that's not standing still, that's the tides.
Crying today doesn't erase the progress. The sand the wave pulled back from is still drier than yesterday's.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'trading my old boo for new shoes / upgrade' caption humor; rewritten with 換季 / 衣櫃斷捨離 wardrobe metaphor

zh-tw.content:
把他從生活裡刪掉，就像換季整理衣櫃。
那件你捨不得丟的，其實早就不合身了，留著只是佔位子。
清空之後，衣櫃跟心裡都多了好多空間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後的斷捨離不只是丟東西，是把不適合的人也一起整理掉。適合傳給那個房間還堆滿前任痕跡、遲遲不肯收的朋友。
variations: 捨不得丟的不一定是好的，常常只是習慣。
衣櫃留位子給新的衣服，心也是。

en.content:
Deleting him from my life felt like a seasonal closet clean-out.
That piece you couldn't bear to toss? It stopped fitting ages ago — it was just taking up space.
Once it's gone, both the closet and my chest have so much more room.
en.editorNote: Post-breakup decluttering isn't just tossing objects — it's sorting out the people who no longer fit. For the friend whose room is still full of ex-traces she won't pack away.
variations: What you can't bear to throw out isn't always good. Usually it's just habit.
A closet leaves space for new clothes. So does a heart.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'some people are part of your history, not your destiny' theme; rewritten with 路過的站 / 終點站 transit metaphor

zh-tw.content:
有些人是你人生的某一站，不是終點站。
你以為要在那裡下車，後來才知道，列車本來就會繼續開。
他是風景，不是目的地。
zh-tw.editorNote: 分手後最難接受的是「他不是命中注定」。傳給那個一直覺得「失去他人生就停了」的朋友——車還在開，你只是少了一個同行的人，不是少了路。
variations: 他是經過的站名，不是你車票上的終點。
下錯站不可怕，可怕的是賴在月台上不肯再上車。

en.content:
Some people are a stop on your journey, not the final destination.
You thought you'd get off there — then you realized the train was always going to keep moving.
They were the scenery, not the place you were headed.
en.editorNote: The hardest thing to accept after a breakup is 'they weren't my destiny.' For the friend who feels life stopped when she lost him — the train's still moving. You lost a fellow passenger, not the road.
variations: They were the name of a station you passed, not the destination on your ticket.
Getting off at the wrong stop isn't the problem. Refusing to board the next train is.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I chased peace, money, and my glow-up' empowerment caption; rewritten with 追蹤清單 / 取消追蹤 social-media metaphor

zh-tw.content:
分手後我沒有去追他，我去追別的東西：
好好睡覺、存錢、還有我自己的發光。
他取消追蹤我沒關係，反正我的更新他已經跟不上了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最有力的姿態不是挽回，是把力氣轉回自己身上。傳給那個剛分手、開始認真過生活的朋友，提醒她重點是往前走，不是回頭追。
variations: 我沒有要他回來，我只是把追他的力氣拿去追更好的生活。
他退追蹤是他的損失，我的版本一直在更新。

en.content:
After the breakup I didn't chase him. I chased other things:
real sleep, savings, and my own glow.
He can unfollow me — he stopped being able to keep up with my updates anyway.
en.editorNote: The most powerful post-breakup move isn't winning them back, it's redirecting that energy into yourself. For the friend who just got serious about her own life — remind her the point is moving forward, not chasing back.
variations: I'm not asking him back. I just took the energy I spent chasing him and chased a better life instead.
Him unfollowing is his loss. My version keeps updating.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'plot twist: I'm now the main character, side quests include self-love and ice cream' caption; rewritten with 支線任務 / 主線 RPG game metaphor

zh-tw.content:
劇情更新：我把那段感情設成「已完成的支線任務」。
獎勵：自由、睡飽、還有一桶冰淇淋。
現在回到主線，主角是我自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把一段過去的感情當成「打完的支線」就不會卡在那裡。傳給那個一直覺得分手是「遊戲結束」的朋友——不是結束，是回到主線。
variations: 他是支線 NPC，不是最終魔王，打完就該往前走。
感情卡關不用一直存檔重來，有些任務做完就該封存。

en.content:
Status update: I marked that relationship as a completed side quest.
Rewards: freedom, full nights of sleep, and a tub of ice cream.
Now back to the main story, where I'm the lead.
en.editorNote: Treating a past relationship as a 'finished side quest' keeps you from getting stuck on it. For the friend who feels a breakup is 'game over' — it's not the end, it's just back to the main quest.
variations: He was a side-quest NPC, not the final boss. Clear it and move on.
You don't have to keep reloading a stuck save. Some quests are meant to be archived once done.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '真正的放下並不是丟掉，而是把曾經好好放進記憶裡' theme; rewritten with 相簿封存 / 不再翻 photo-album metaphor

zh-tw.content:
真正的放下，不是把回憶刪掉，
是把它收進相簿最後一頁，標記日期，然後不再每天翻。
它還在，只是你已經不靠它過日子了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 放下不等於遺忘。傳給那個以為「要徹底忘記才算走出來」的朋友——你可以記得，只是不再痛了，那才是真的好了。
variations: 釋懷不是清空，是你終於可以把那段放在角落，不用一直拿出來看。
你不用假裝沒愛過，你只要不再用那段定義現在的自己。

en.content:
Real letting go isn't deleting the memories.
It's filing them on the last page of the album, dating them, and no longer flipping back every day.
They're still there — you just don't live off them anymore.
en.editorNote: Letting go isn't forgetting. For the friend who thinks 'I have to completely forget to move on' — you can remember and still not hurt. That's what healed actually looks like.
variations: Moving on isn't wiping the slate. It's finally being able to leave that chapter in the corner without pulling it out constantly.
You don't have to pretend you never loved them. You just stop letting it define who you are now.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'You were my cup of tea, but I drink champagne now' caption; rewritten with 手搖飲 / 升級 Taiwan-friendly drink metaphor

zh-tw.content:
你曾經是我的微糖少冰，喝起來剛剛好。
後來才發現，我其實值得一杯料多到滿出來的。
謝謝你讓我知道，我可以加價升級。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把前任當成「喝過但不再點的那杯」最不傷人。適合放在 IG 限時，宣告自己換口味、過得更好。
variations: 他不是不好喝，是我長大了，嘴變刁了。
下一杯我要自己選料，不再將就別人幫我點的。

en.content:
You used to be my half-sugar, light-ice — just right at the time.
Then I realized I deserve the cup that's overflowing with toppings.
Thanks for teaching me I'm allowed to upgrade.
en.editorNote: Framing an ex as 'the drink I tried but no longer order' keeps it painless. Great for an IG story announcing you've changed your taste and you're doing better.
variations: He wasn't bad — I just grew up, and my taste got pickier.
Next cup, I'm picking my own toppings. No more settling for what someone else ordered for me.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '有些人來過你的世界，只是為了教會你如何告別' theme; rewritten with 教練 / 結業 fitness-class metaphor

zh-tw.content:
有些人走進你的人生，不是來陪你到老，是來當你的教練。
他教會你怎麼設界線、怎麼說不、怎麼好好告別。
課上完了，他下課了，但你練出來的肌肉是你自己的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把一段傷過你的感情當成「訓練課程」，痛就有了意義。傳給那個一直問「為什麼遇到他」的朋友——不是來折磨你，是來把你練強。
variations: 他是來教你功課的，不是來陪你寫一輩子作業的。
課程很硬，但結業之後的你，已經不是進場時那個人了。

en.content:
Some people walk into your life not to grow old with you, but to be your coach.
They teach you how to set boundaries, how to say no, how to say goodbye well.
The class ends, they clock out — but the muscle you built is yours to keep.
en.editorNote: Treating a relationship that hurt you as a 'training course' gives the pain meaning. For the friend asking 'why did I have to meet him?' — not to torment you, but to make you stronger.
variations: They came to teach you the lesson, not to do the homework with you forever.
The course was brutal, but the you who graduates isn't the same person who walked in.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'Love sucks. Pizza doesn't.' funny caption; rewritten with 滷味 / 從不已讀 Taiwan-friendly food framing

zh-tw.content:
愛情會讓你失望，滷味不會。
你選什麼它就給你什麼，從不挑食，也從不已讀。
這大概就是我目前最穩定的關係。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最不需要講道理的時刻，就配一份宵夜剛剛好。傳給剛分手的朋友，附上滷味照片殺傷力加倍。
variations: 感情會背叛你，但滷味老闆永遠記得你不要香菜。
下一段感情，我希望對方至少有滷味那麼懂我。

en.content:
Love lets you down. Late-night takeout doesn't.
You pick what you want, it never judges, and it never leaves you on read.
Honestly, it's the most stable relationship I've got right now.
en.editorNote: The post-breakup moment that needs zero life advice — just a midnight snack. Send to a freshly single friend with a food photo attached for double impact.
variations: Love might betray you, but the takeout place always remembers your order.
Next relationship, I just want someone who gets me the way my dinner does.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'From us to me, this solo chapter looks really good' caption; rewritten with 單人套餐 / 加大 dining-for-one metaphor

zh-tw.content:
以前點餐都要先問他要吃什麼，
現在我直接點單人套餐，還加大。
原來一個人吃飯不是孤單，是終於可以全部都照自己的意思。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最被低估的自由，是不用再配合別人的口味。傳給那個剛開始一個人生活、慢慢找回自己節奏的朋友。
variations: 從「我們要吃什麼」到「我想吃什麼」，這一步走得很爽。
一個人的餐桌沒有比較冷，是終於沒有人跟我搶最後一塊。

en.content:
I used to ask him what he wanted before ordering.
Now I just get the meal-for-one — and size it up.
Turns out eating alone isn't lonely. It's finally getting everything exactly my way.
en.editorNote: The most underrated post-breakup freedom is never accommodating someone else's taste again. For the friend starting solo life and slowly finding her own rhythm.
variations: Going from 'what should we eat' to 'what do I want' felt amazing.
A table for one isn't colder. There's just nobody fighting me for the last bite.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '放下，才是最好的不打擾' theme; rewritten with 不再查勤 / 不再對號入座 daily-behavior framing

zh-tw.content:
我知道我真的放下了，是因為——
他發限時動態的時候，我沒有想對號入座；
他換了大頭貼，我也沒有想知道是誰拍的。
不是不在乎，是終於把注意力收回給自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 釋懷不是大聲宣告，是那些小動作默默停了。傳給那個還在每天分析前任貼文的朋友——等你哪天懶得看了，就真的好了。
variations: 放下的證據不是刪好友，是你連點開都覺得沒必要。
真正走出來的那天很安靜，安靜到你自己都差點沒發現。

en.content:
I knew I'd really let go when —
he posted a story and I didn't try to read myself into it;
he changed his profile photo and I didn't wonder who took it.
It's not that I stopped caring. I just finally pulled my attention back to myself.
en.editorNote: Moving on isn't a loud announcement — it's the small habits quietly stopping. For the friend still analyzing her ex's posts daily: the day you can't be bothered to look is the day you're actually fine.
variations: Proof you've let go isn't unfriending them — it's not even feeling the need to click.
The day you truly move on is quiet. So quiet you almost miss it yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by savage 'thanks for the downgrade lesson' caption format; rewritten with 試用期 / 沒過 probation-period workplace metaphor

zh-tw.content:
他沒有甩我，是試用期沒過，被我資遣了。
表現評估：守時零分、溝通零分、情緒穩定度有待加強。
感謝應徵，但這個職缺我決定空著，自己一個人也能營運。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把分手當成「人資決策」最不傷自尊。傳給那個被分手後自我懷疑的朋友——不是你不夠好，是他試用期表現不及格。
variations: 他不是離職，是我考核沒讓他轉正。
這個職缺我寧可開天窗，也不要再找不適任的人。

en.content:
He didn't dump me. He failed probation, so I let him go.
Performance review: punctuality zero, communication zero, emotional stability needs improvement.
Thanks for applying, but I'm leaving this position open — I run just fine solo.
en.editorNote: Framing a breakup as an HR decision keeps your dignity intact. For the friend doubting herself after being dumped — it wasn't that you weren't enough. He just flunked his trial period.
variations: He didn't quit. I declined to make him permanent.
I'd rather leave the role empty than hire someone unqualified again.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '學會放下，擁抱更好的自己' theme; rewritten with 換季 / 收進衣櫃 seasonal-wardrobe metaphor

zh-tw.content:
放下一個人就像換季收衣服。
你不是討厭那件外套，是天氣變了，它不再適合現在的你。
把它好好摺起來收進衣櫃，不用丟掉，也不用每天穿在身上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 放下不必恨，也不必勉強遺忘。傳給那個覺得「不討厭他就代表還沒走出來」的朋友——你可以平靜地收好，那才是真的長大了。
variations: 有些感情像厚外套，陪你過了一個冬天，季節到了就該收起來。
你不用把回憶燒掉，只要不再每天披在身上就好。

en.content:
Letting someone go is like packing away clothes when the season changes.
It's not that you hate the coat — the weather shifted, and it no longer suits who you are now.
Fold it neatly, tuck it in the closet. You don't have to throw it out, and you don't have to wear it every day.
en.editorNote: Letting go doesn't require hatred or forced forgetting. For the friend who thinks 'not hating him means I haven't moved on' — you can pack it away calmly. That's what growing up actually looks like.
variations: Some relationships are like winter coats — they got you through one season, and then it's time to put them away.
You don't have to burn the memories. You just stop wearing them every single day.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'if my ex calls I'll hang up like a horror movie' savage caption; rewritten with 來電顯示 / 詐騙集團 phone-scam localization

zh-tw.content:
他現在打來，我接起來的反應跟接到詐騙電話一樣——
「您好，我這邊沒有您要的服務，請不要再撥打這支號碼。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把前任歸類成「詐騙來電」最解氣。傳給那個還會手抖接前任電話的朋友，提醒她該封鎖就封鎖。
variations: 他的來電顯示我已經改成「拒接」，比封鎖溫柔一點，但效果一樣。
以前看到他打來會心跳加速，現在只想按擴音放給朋友聽笑話。

en.content:
If he calls now, I answer it exactly like I'd answer a scam call —
"Hello, we don't offer the service you're looking for. Please stop dialing this number."
en.editorNote: Filing your ex under 'spam caller' is the pettiest kind of peace. For the friend whose hands still shake when his name pops up — remind her the block button exists.
variations: I renamed his caller ID to 'Decline.' Gentler than a block, same result.
His call used to make my heart race. Now I just want to put it on speaker so my friends can laugh.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'growth begins where love ends' healing theme; rewritten with 房間重新裝潢 / 騰出空間 home-renovation metaphor

zh-tw.content:
他搬走以後，我把那個房間重新裝潢。
原來不是房子變大了，是我終於把擺給別人的位置，留給了自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後騰出來的空間，不是缺口，是可以重新規劃的自由。傳給那個剛恢復單身、開始重新佈置生活的朋友。
variations: 他留下的不是空位，是讓我終於可以放自己喜歡的東西的地方。
清空一個人之後，我才看見原來這個空間一直都是我的。

en.content:
After he moved out, I redecorated that room.
Turns out the place didn't get bigger — I just finally gave the space I'd been saving for someone else back to myself.
en.editorNote: The space a breakup clears isn't a hole — it's the freedom to redesign. For the friend who just went single and started rearranging her whole life.
variations: What he left behind wasn't an empty seat. It was room to finally put my own things.
After I cleared a person out, I realized the space was always mine to begin with.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'thanks for the expensive lesson' breakup pun format; rewritten with 學費 / 補習班 Taiwan cram-school metaphor

zh-tw.content:
這段感情很貴，但我把它當學費繳了。
主修「怎麼看人」，補考過了，畢業證書上寫著：以後不選這種的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把分手的代價當成「學費」，痛就變成投資。傳給那個一直懊惱「浪費了好幾年」的朋友——你不是賠錢，你是拿到了一張很實用的證書。
variations: 學費繳得有點貴，但這堂課我這輩子都不會再上第二次。
他教會我的事，比情話值錢多了。

en.content:
That relationship was expensive, but I filed it under tuition.
Major: reading people. I passed the makeup exam, and my diploma reads: never pick this type again.
en.editorNote: Treating the cost of a breakup as 'tuition' turns the pain into an investment. For the friend agonizing over 'wasted years' — you didn't lose money, you earned a very practical certificate.
variations: The tuition stung, but it's a class I'll never have to retake.
What he taught me was worth a lot more than any sweet talk.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'healing begins within' self-love theme; rewritten with 慢火燉湯 / 不能催 slow-cooking metaphor

zh-tw.content:
復原像燉一鍋湯，急不得。
你越想開大火快點好，越容易燒焦。
慢慢來，時間到了，自然會香。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀復原最忌諱「逼自己趕快好」。傳給那個一直催自己「怎麼還沒走出來」的朋友——你沒有慢，你只是還在燉。
variations: 好起來沒有快轉鍵，火候到了它自己會熟。
別人問你好了沒，你可以說：還在燉，香味快出來了。

en.content:
Healing is like simmering a pot of soup — you can't rush it.
The harder you crank the heat to finish faster, the more likely you are to burn it.
Take your time. When it's ready, the aroma comes on its own.
en.editorNote: The worst thing in breakup recovery is forcing yourself to 'hurry up and be okay.' For the friend nagging herself with 'why am I not over it yet' — you're not slow, you're still simmering.
variations: There's no fast-forward button for healing. When the heat's had enough time, it's done.
When people ask if you're over it, you can say: still simmering, the smell's almost there.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'single and ready to eat a whole pizza' funny caption format; rewritten with Taiwan 一個人火鍋 / 不用分料 localization

zh-tw.content:
單身又自由？
比較像是單身又可以一個人吃整鍋火鍋，
不用把蛋餃分給別人，也沒人偷夾我的肉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後第一個真正的快樂時刻，常常是發現「終於不用配合別人的口味」。傳給剛恢復單身的朋友，附一張一人鍋的照片更好笑。
variations: 分手最大的好處：火鍋料終於全部都是我的。
我不是孤單，我只是終於可以照自己的順序下菜。

en.content:
Single and free?
More like single and finally eating a whole hot pot alone —
no one to split the dumplings with, no one stealing my beef.
en.editorNote: The first real moment of joy after a breakup is often realizing you no longer have to match someone else's taste. Send to a newly single friend with a photo of a hot pot for one.
variations: Best part of the breakup: all the hot pot ingredients are finally mine.
I'm not lonely. I just get to cook everything in my own order now.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'love is blind but my breakup goggles are clear' caption; rewritten with 近視 / 度數對了 eyesight metaphor

zh-tw.content:
他們說愛情讓人盲目，我現在懂了。
分手之後我的視力突然回正常，
才看清楚他根本不是我想像的那個樣子——是我之前度數配錯了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後看清對方真面目，其實是好事。傳給那個總說「他以前不是這樣」的朋友——他一直都是這樣，只是你現在度數對了。
variations: 戀愛的時候我是高度近視，分手以後突然 1.0。
不是他變了，是我終於把濾鏡拿下來看清楚了。

en.content:
They say love is blind. Now I get it.
After the breakup, my eyesight snapped back to normal,
and I finally saw he was never who I imagined — my prescription was just wrong before.
en.editorNote: Seeing your ex clearly after a breakup is actually a good thing. For the friend who keeps saying 'he wasn't like this before' — he always was, your vision just corrected.
variations: In love I had terrible eyesight. Post-breakup, suddenly 20/20.
He didn't change. I just finally took off the filter and looked.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '放下，才是最好的不打擾' canonical line; rewritten with 把鑰匙還回去 / 不再守門 letting-go metaphor

zh-tw.content:
放下不是把他從心裡趕出去，
是把那扇你一直幫他留著的門關上，
然後把鑰匙還給自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 很多人放不下，是因為一直幫對方留著回來的位置。傳給那個還在「萬一他回來」的朋友——門關上不是恨，是你決定不再當守門的人。
variations: 你不是忘不了他，你只是還沒願意把那扇門上鎖。
釋懷是把等他的那把鑰匙，重新插回自己的門。

en.content:
Letting go isn't kicking them out of your heart.
It's closing the door you kept holding open for them,
and handing the key back to yourself.
en.editorNote: Many people can't let go because they keep a spot saved for the other person to come back to. For the friend still on 'what if they return' — closing the door isn't hatred, it's deciding to stop being the gatekeeper.
variations: You haven't failed to forget them. You just haven't been willing to lock the door yet.
Moving on is taking the key you saved for them and putting it back in your own door.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I lost in love but gained in freedom' empowerment line; rewritten with 對帳單 / 淨賺 accounting metaphor

zh-tw.content:
這段感情結算一下：
失去一個不珍惜我的人，賺回整個週末、所有的睡眠，還有對自己的話語權。
怎麼算都是淨賺。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後做一次「盤點」，常常會發現自己其實賺到了。傳給那個只盯著「失去」的朋友，提醒她另一欄寫滿了「賺回來」。
variations: 分手對帳單：支出一個前任，收入一整個自由。
別只看你失去什麼，看看你拿回了多少時間。

en.content:
Let's settle the accounts on this relationship:
Lost one person who didn't value me. Gained back my whole weekend, all my sleep, and the final say over my own life.
Net profit, any way you run the numbers.
en.editorNote: Doing an 'inventory' after a breakup often reveals you actually came out ahead. For the friend fixated on the 'lost' column — remind her the other column is full of things she got back.
variations: Breakup balance sheet: expense, one ex. Income, a whole lot of freedom.
Don't just count what you lost. Count how much time you got back.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'whole snack, don't need anybody stealing my fries' humor caption; rewritten with 整份套餐 / 加價 Taiwan food metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我本人就是一整份套餐，主餐、副餐、飲料都齊。
誰想分一口薯條都得加價，更別說想白吃整份的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最重要的是把自我價值喊回來。傳給那個總覺得「沒人要」的朋友——不是沒人要，是你定價太佛心了。
variations: 我不是缺一塊的拼圖，我本來就是完整的那幅畫。
想吃我這份套餐，請先看清楚菜單價格。

en.content:
I'm a full combo meal — main, sides, and a drink, all included.
Want to steal a fry? That's extra. Want the whole thing for free? Keep dreaming.
en.editorNote: After a breakup, the key move is reclaiming your own worth. For the friend who feels 'nobody wants me' — they do, you were just priced way too generously.
variations: I'm not a puzzle missing a piece. I was always the whole picture.
Want a taste of this combo? Check the price on the menu first.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'exchanged my better half for a better peace' empowerment line; rewritten with 退貨 / 換購 consumer metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我把「另一半」退貨了，換購了一份「全部的自己」。
結果發現——後者本來就不用湊，我一個人就是滿件。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀後最大的覺醒：你從來不是半個人，不需要誰來把你補滿。傳給那個總說「我需要找到我的另一半」的朋友。
variations: 我不缺另一半，我缺的是早點發現自己是完整的。
退掉一個不合適的，換回一個一直都在的自己。

en.content:
I returned my 'better half' and exchanged it for a whole self.
Turns out — the second one was never on sale. I was always one complete piece.
en.editorNote: The biggest post-breakup awakening: you were never half a person who needed completing. For the friend always saying 'I need to find my other half.'
variations: I don't need a better half. I just needed to notice sooner that I was already whole.
Returned the one that didn't fit, got back the self that was here all along.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'thank goodness for small miracles / never find someone like him' savage caption; rewritten with 限量 / 缺貨 ironic framing

zh-tw.content:
他說我這輩子再也找不到像他這樣的人。
我說對啊，謝天謝地，這款已經停產了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 嗆得漂亮又不失優雅，適合分手後第一篇限時動態。重點是讓人覺得你已經過得很好，而且很慶幸。
variations: 再也找不到像他這樣的人？這正是我換手機號碼的原因。
他是限量款沒錯，限量到我一個就受夠了。

en.content:
He said I'd never find anyone like him again.
I said — thank goodness, that model's been discontinued.
en.editorNote: Savage but still classy, perfect for the first IG story after a breakup. The point is letting everyone see you're doing great and counting your blessings.
variations: Never find anyone like him again? That's exactly why I changed my number.
He's a limited edition, all right — so limited that one was already too many.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '放下不是丟掉，是好好放進記憶裡' theme; rewritten with 收進抽屜 / 不上鎖 gentle-closure metaphor

zh-tw.content:
放下不是把你丟掉，是把那段日子好好摺好，收進抽屜。
我沒上鎖，只是再也沒有打開的理由了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 成熟的釋懷不是恨，也不是刪除，是溫柔地收好。傳給那個怕「放下＝背叛回憶」的朋友——你可以好好保留，也可以好好往前。
variations: 我沒有忘記你，我只是不再需要時時想起。
把回憶收起來，不是因為它不重要，是因為我終於有別的事可以做了。

en.content:
Letting go isn't throwing you away. It's folding those days neatly and tucking them in a drawer.
I didn't lock it — I just no longer have a reason to open it.
en.editorNote: Mature closure isn't hatred or deletion — it's gentle storage. For the friend afraid that 'moving on means betraying the memories' — you can keep them carefully and still move forward.
variations: I didn't forget you. I just no longer need to remember you all the time.
I put the memories away not because they don't matter, but because I finally have other things to do.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'breakup completed, self-respect upgraded, standards raised' empowerment caption; rewritten with 版本更新 / 標準提高 framing distinct from existing reboot item

zh-tw.content:
分手完成。自尊心：升級。
標準：永久調高，不再開放下修。
下一個想進來的，請先符合最低門檻。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失戀不是降級，是把標準調回它本來該有的高度。傳給那個曾經為了留住對方一再讓步的朋友——這次別再打折了。
variations: 這次分手最大的收穫，是我終於知道自己值得什麼。
標準不是難搞，是我終於肯尊重自己。

en.content:
Breakup: complete. Self-respect: upgraded.
Standards: permanently raised, no longer open to negotiation.
Next applicant, please meet the minimum requirements first.
en.editorNote: A breakup isn't a downgrade — it's resetting your standards back to where they should've been. For the friend who kept lowering the bar to hold onto someone. No more discounts this time.
variations: The biggest win from this breakup: I finally know what I deserve.
High standards aren't me being difficult. They're me finally respecting myself.
